file.content:
library(purrr)
library(testthat)
library(assertthat)

gmask5 <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2")
mask <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask2.nii.gz", package="neuroim2")

context("neurovecseq")

test_that("can construct a NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  expect_equal(dim(cvec), dim(vs))
  expect_equal(space(cvec), space(vs))

})

test_that("can linearly index a NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  expect_equal(vs[1], cvec[1])
  for (i in 1:20) {
    idx <- sample(1:prod(dim(cvec)), 50)
    expect_equal(vs[idx], cvec[idx])
  }
})

test_that("can array index a NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  expect_equal(vs[1], cvec[1])

  expect_equal(vs[1,,,], cvec[1,,,])
  expect_equal(vs[1,2,,], cvec[1,2,,])
  expect_equal(vs[1,2,3,], cvec[1,2,3,])
  expect_equal(vs[1,2,3,4], cvec[1,2,3,4])
  expect_equal(vs[1:2,2,3,4], cvec[1:2,2,3,4])
  expect_equal(vs[1:2,2,3,3:4], cvec[1:2,2,3,3:4])
  expect_equal(vs[1:2,2,,3:4], cvec[1:2,2,,3:4])
  expect_equal(vs[1:2,,,3:4], cvec[1:2,,,3:4])
  expect_equal(vs[,,,3:4], cvec[,,,3:4])

})

test_that("can extract vectors from NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  v1 <- vs %>% vectors()
  v2 <- cvec %>% vectors()
  checkind <- sample(1:length(v1), 100)
  for (i in checkind) {
    expect_equal(v1[[i]], v2[[i]])
  }

})

test_that("can extract vols from NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  v1 <- vs %>% vols()
  v2 <- cvec %>% vols()
  for (i in length(v1)) {
    expect_equal(v1[[i]], v2[[i]])
  }

})


test_that("can map over vols from NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  out <- do.call(concat, vs %>% vols() %>% purrr::map( ~ .))
  #out <- do.call(concat, vlist)
  expect_true(all(as.vector(cvec) == as.vector(out)))
})

test_that("can subset a NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(c(gmask5, gmask5, gmask5))
  vec5 <- sub_vector(vec, 1:4)
  vec2 <- read_vec(gmask5)
  expect_true(all(vec5[] == vec2[]))
})

test_that("can read a sparse NeuroVecSeq", {
  m <- read_vol(mask)
  vec <- read_vec(c(gmask5, gmask5, gmask5), mask=as.logical(m))
  vec5 <- sub_vector(vec, 1:4)
  vec2 <- read_vec(gmask5, mask=as.logical(m))

  ## TODO check that values are same
  expect_true(all(dim(vec5) == dim(vec2)))
})

test_that("can use a MappedNeuroVec as elements in a NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)

  mvecs <- replicate(3, MappedNeuroVec(gmask5), simplify=FALSE)
  vs2 <- do.call(NeuroVecSeq, mvecs)


  v1 <- vs %>% vols()
  v2 <- vs2 %>% vols()

  for (i in 1:length(v1)) {
    expect_equal(v1[[i]], v2[[i]])
  }

  v1 <- vs %>% vectors()
  v2 <- vs2 %>% vectors()
  checkind <- sample(1:length(v1), 100)
  for (i in checkind) {
    expect_equal(v1[[i]], v2[[i]])
  }

})

test_that("series() works when NeuroVecSeq has SparseNeuroVec", {
  # 1) Read a reference dense vec
  dense_vec <- read_vec(gmask5)  # this is your 4D reference data

  # 2) Create a mask and re-read the same data => yields SparseNeuroVec
  mvol <- read_vol(mask)
  sparse_vec <- read_vec(gmask5, mask = as.logical(mvol))  # now a SparseNeuroVec

  # 3) Build a NeuroVecSeq from repeated sparse vectors
  s1 <- sparse_vec
  s2 <- sparse_vec
  s3 <- sparse_vec
  seq_sparse <- NeuroVecSeq(s1, s2, s3)

  # 4) Build a single 'concat' version as reference
  concat_sparse <- concat(s1, s2, s3)

  # Basic dimension checks
  expect_equal(dim(seq_sparse), dim(concat_sparse))
  expect_equal(space(seq_sparse), space(concat_sparse))

  # 5) Test random voxel indices in 'series'
  set.seed(123)
  idx <- sample(seq_len(prod(dim(concat_sparse)[1:3])), 25)  # 25 random voxel indices

  mat_seq <- series(seq_sparse, idx)
  mat_con <- series(concat_sparse, idx)
  expect_equal(dim(mat_seq), dim(mat_con))
  expect_equal(mat_seq, mat_con)

  # 6) Check single-voxel extraction with drop=TRUE => should yield a time vector
  vox <- idx[1]  # pick one voxel
  vec_seq <- series(seq_sparse, vox, drop=TRUE)
  vec_con <- series(concat_sparse, vox, drop=TRUE)

  expect_true(is.numeric(vec_seq) && is.vector(vec_seq))
  expect_equal(length(vec_seq), dim(concat_sparse)[4])
  expect_equal(vec_seq, vec_con)
})

context("vignette neuro vectors")

test_that("test_create_neurovec works correctly", {
  # Load example data
  file_name <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2")
  
  # Read single file as NeuroVec
  vec <- read_vec(file_name)
  expect_true(inherits(vec, "NeuroVec"))
  expect_equal(length(dim(vec)), 4)
  
  # Read multiple files
  vec_multi <- read_vec(c(file_name, file_name, file_name))
  expect_equal(dim(vec_multi)[4], 12)  # 3 files * 4 volumes each
  
  # Test sub_vector
  vec_subset <- sub_vector(vec_multi, 1:6)
  expect_equal(dim(vec_subset)[4], 6)
  
  # Test series extraction
  ts <- series(vec, 1, 1, 1)
  expect_true(is.numeric(ts))
  expect_equal(length(ts), dim(vec)[4])
  
  # Test series_roi with spherical ROI
  vol <- read_vol(file_name)
  roi <- spherical_roi(vol, c(12, 12, 12), radius=8)
  rvec <- series_roi(vec, roi)
  expect_true(inherits(rvec, "ROIVec"))
  
  # Test series_roi with indices
  rvec_idx <- series_roi(vec, 1:100)
  expect_true(inherits(rvec_idx, "ROIVec"))
  expect_equal(nrow(coords(rvec_idx)), 100)
  
  # Test series with indices
  mat <- series(vec, 1:100)
  expect_true(is.matrix(mat))
  expect_equal(nrow(mat), dim(vec)[4])  # series returns time x voxels
  expect_equal(ncol(mat), 100)
  
  # Test series with voxel coordinates
  mask <- read_vol(file_name)
  vox <- index_to_grid(mask, 1:100)
  mat_vox <- series(vec, vox)
  expect_true(is.matrix(mat_vox))
  expect_equal(nrow(mat_vox), dim(vec)[4])  # series returns time x voxels
  expect_equal(ncol(mat_vox), 100)
})

context("vignette pipelines")

library(purrr)

test_that("test_searchlight_statistics works correctly", {
  # Load example volume
  file_name <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2")
  vol <- read_vol(file_name)
  mask.idx <- which(vol > 0)
  
  # Create test volume with random values
  vol2 <- vol
  vol2[mask.idx] <- runif(length(mask.idx))
  
  # Test searchlight with eager=TRUE for local standard deviation
  sdvol_values <- vol %>% 
    searchlight(radius=5, eager=TRUE) %>% 
    map_dbl(~ sd(values(.)))
  
  sdvol <- NeuroVol(sdvol_values, space=space(vol), indices=which(vol != 0))
  expect_true(inherits(sdvol, "NeuroVol"))
  expect_equal(length(sdvol_values), length(which(vol != 0)))
})

test_that("test_4d_processing works correctly", {
  # Load example volume and create a 4D NeuroVec
  file_name <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2")
  vol <- read_vol(file_name)
  
  # Create a 4D volume by concatenating
  vec <- concat(vol, vol, vol, vol, vol)
  expect_true(inherits(vec, "NeuroVec"))
  expect_equal(dim(vec)[4], 5)
  
  # Map over volumes
  mean_vec <- vec %>% vols() %>% map_dbl(~ mean(values(.)))
  expect_equal(length(mean_vec), 5)
  
  sd_vec <- vec %>% vols() %>% map_dbl(~ sd(values(.)))
  expect_equal(length(sd_vec), 5)
  
  # Map over vectors (time series at each voxel)
  mean_vol_values <- vec %>% vectors() %>% map_dbl(~ mean(.))
  mean_vol <- NeuroVol(mean_vol_values, space=space(vol))
  expect_true(inherits(mean_vol, "NeuroVol"))
  expect_equal(dim(mean_vol), dim(vol)[1:3])
})

test_that("test_knn_smoothing works correctly", {
  # Load example volume
  file_name <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2")
  vol <- read_vol(file_name)
  mask.idx <- which(vol > 0)
  
  # Create test volume with random values
  vol2 <- vol
  vol2[mask.idx] <- runif(length(mask.idx))
  
  # KNN smoothing using searchlight
  k <- 12
  knnfvol_values <- vol2 %>% 
    searchlight(radius=6, eager=TRUE) %>% 
    map_dbl(function(x) {
      # Get values from the ROI
      roi_values <- values(x)
      center_idx <- x@center_index
      
      # For ROIVolWindow, center_index refers to position in the ROI
      # We need to handle this differently
      if (length(roi_values) > 0) {
        mean(roi_values)
      } else {
        NA_real_
      }
    })
  
  # Remove NAs and create volume
  valid_idx <- !is.na(knnfvol_values)
  knnfvol <- NeuroVol(
    knnfvol_values[valid_idx], 
    space=space(vol), 
    indices=which(vol != 0)[valid_idx]
  )
  
  expect_true(inherits(knnfvol, "NeuroVol"))
  expect_true(sum(!is.na(values(knnfvol))) > 0)
})

file.content:
context("vignette roi")

library(purrr)

test_that("test_searchlight works correctly", {
  # Load example volume
  file_name <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask2.nii.gz", package="neuroim2")
  vol <- read_vol(file_name)
  
  # Test exhaustive searchlight with eager=TRUE
  slist <- searchlight(vol, radius=8, eager=TRUE)
  expect_true(is.list(slist))
  expect_true(length(slist) > 0)
  
  # Compute mean values in each searchlight
  ret <- slist %>% purrr::map_dbl(~ mean(vol[coords(.)]))
  expect_equal(length(ret), length(slist))
  expect_true(all(!is.na(ret)))
  
  # Test random searchlight
  ret_random <- vol %>% 
    random_searchlight(radius=8) %>% 
    purrr::map_dbl(~ mean(vol[coords(.)]))
  expect_true(length(ret_random) > 0)
  expect_true(all(!is.na(ret_random)))
  
  # Test clustered searchlight
  grid <- index_to_coord(vol, which(vol > 0))
  kres <- kmeans(grid, centers=50, iter.max=500)
  kvol <- ClusteredNeuroVol(vol, kres$cluster)
  
  ret_clustered <- vol %>% 
    clustered_searchlight(cvol=kvol) %>% 
    purrr::map_dbl(~ mean(vol[coords(.)]))
  expect_equal(length(ret_clustered), 50)
  expect_true(all(!is.na(ret_clustered)))
  
  # Test searchlight_coords
  coords_list <- searchlight_coords(vol, radius=4, nonzero=FALSE)
  expect_true(inherits(coords_list, "deflist"))
  
  # Force evaluation of a few elements to test
  first_coords <- coords_list[[1]]
  expect_true(is.matrix(first_coords))
  expect_equal(ncol(first_coords), 3)
})

## Summary of R Test Files

### 1. `/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/neuroim2/tests/testthat/test-splitscale.R`

**Main functionality being tested:**
- `split_reduce()` function - splits a matrix by a factor and applies a reduction function
- `split_scale()` function - splits a matrix by a factor and applies scaling (centering and standardization)

**Key test cases:**
- Tests that `split_reduce` correctly splits a matrix by factor levels and applies a custom sum function
- Tests that `split_scale` correctly splits a matrix by factor levels and applies centering and scaling independently to each group

**Specific edge cases:**
- Uses a simple 4x3 matrix with factor splitting rows into two groups (1,1,2,2)
- Verifies that scaling is applied separately to each factor group rather than to the entire matrix

### 2. `/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/neuroim2/tests/testthat/test-vecseq.R`

**Main functionality being tested:**
- `NeuroVecSeq` class - a sequence of neuroimaging vectors
- Various indexing and subsetting operations
- Interaction with sparse and mapped neuroimaging data structures

**Key test cases:**
- Construction of NeuroVecSeq from multiple NeuroVec objects
- Linear indexing (single index access)
- Array indexing (multi-dimensional access with various combinations)
- Extraction of vectors and volumes
- Mapping operations over volumes
- Subsetting operations
- Working with sparse data (using masks)
- Using MappedNeuroVec as elements
- Series extraction from sparse NeuroVecSeq

**Specific edge cases:**
- Random sampling of indices for thorough testing
- Various array indexing patterns including partial indices (e.g., `[1:2,,,3:4]`)
- Sparse data handling with masks
- Memory-mapped file handling
- Single voxel extraction with `drop=TRUE`

### 3. `/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/neuroim2/tests/testthat/test-vignette-neuro-vectors.R`

**Main functionality being tested:**
- Basic NeuroVec operations as documented in vignettes
- File reading and vector creation
- ROI (Region of Interest) operations
- Time series extraction

**Key test cases:**
- Reading single and multiple neuroimaging files
- Creating subsets of vectors
- Extracting time series from specific voxels
- Creating spherical ROIs
- Extracting data using ROI specifications
- Converting voxel indices to grid coordinates

**Specific edge cases:**
- Multiple file concatenation (testing 3 files concatenated)
- Both index-based and coordinate-based data extraction
- Spherical ROI with specific radius (8 voxels)

### 4. `/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/neuroim2/tests/testthat/test-vignette-pipelines.R`

**Main functionality being tested:**
- Searchlight statistics computation
- 4D neuroimaging data processing pipelines
- K-nearest neighbor smoothing

**Key test cases:**
- Searchlight analysis with local statistics (standard deviation)
- Processing 4D volumes by mapping over time series
- Computing mean and standard deviation across volumes
- KNN-based spatial smoothing

**Specific edge cases:**
- Handling of non-zero mask indices
- Random value generation for testing
- Handling of NA values in smoothing operations
- ROIVolWindow center index handling

### 5. `/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/neuroim2/tests/testthat/test-vignette-roi.R`

**Main functionality being tested:**
- Comprehensive searchlight operations
- Different searchlight strategies (exhaustive, random, clustered)
- Coordinate-based searchlight operations

**Key test cases:**
- Exhaustive searchlight with eager evaluation
- Random searchlight sampling
- Clustered searchlight using k-means clustering
- Searchlight coordinate generation
- Lazy evaluation with deflist

**Specific edge cases:**
- K-means clustering with 50 centers and 500 iterations
- Testing both zero and non-zero voxel handling
- Lazy evaluation of coordinate lists
- Various searchlight radii (4 and 8 voxels)

Each test file focuses on different aspects of the neuroim2 package, from basic data manipulation (splitscale) to complex neuroimaging operations (searchlight analysis, ROI extraction, and 4D data processing). The tests thoroughly cover both basic functionality and edge cases, ensuring robust handling of neuroimaging data structures.

library(testthat)

# Test ClusteredNeuroVol function
test_that("ClusteredNeuroVol works correctly", {
  bspace <- NeuroSpace(c(16, 16, 16), spacing = c(1, 1, 1))
  grid <- index_to_grid(bspace, 1:(16 * 16 * 16))
  kres <- kmeans(grid, centers = 10)
  mask <- NeuroVol(rep(1, 16^3), bspace)
  clusvol <- ClusteredNeuroVol(mask, kres$cluster)

  expect_s4_class(clusvol, "ClusteredNeuroVol")
  # Add more tests to check the correctness of the output, e.g., dimensions, etc.
})

# Test as.DenseNeuroVol function
test_that("as.DenseNeuroVol works correctly", {
  bspace <- NeuroSpace(c(16, 16, 16), spacing = c(1, 1, 1))
  grid <- index_to_grid(bspace, 1:(16 * 16 * 16))
  kres <- kmeans(grid, centers = 10)
  mask <- NeuroVol(rep(1, 16^3), bspace)
  clusvol <- ClusteredNeuroVol(mask, kres$cluster)
  # Use the previously created clusvol object from the ClusteredNeuroVol test
  dense_vol <- as(clusvol, "DenseNeuroVol")

  expect_s4_class(dense_vol, "DenseNeuroVol")
  # Add more tests to check the correctness of the output, e.g., dimensions, etc.
})

# Test show method for ClusteredNeuroVol
test_that("show method for ClusteredNeuroVol works correctly", {
  bspace <- NeuroSpace(c(16, 16, 16), spacing = c(1, 1, 1))
  grid <- index_to_grid(bspace, 1:(16 * 16 * 16))
  kres <- kmeans(grid, centers = 10)
  mask <- NeuroVol(rep(1, 16^3), bspace)
  clusvol <- ClusteredNeuroVol(mask, kres$cluster)
  # Capture the output of the show method
  output <- capture.output(show(clusvol))

  # Check if the output contains the necessary information
  expect_true(any(grepl("NeuroVol", output)))
  expect_true(any(grepl("Type", output)))
  # Add more tests to check the correctness of the output
})

# Test centroids function
test_that("centroids works correctly", {
  bspace <- NeuroSpace(c(16, 16, 16), spacing = c(1, 1, 1))
  grid <- index_to_grid(bspace, 1:(16 * 16 * 16))
  kres <- kmeans(grid, centers = 10)
  mask <- NeuroVol(rep(1, 16^3), bspace)

context("image processing")


test_that("can gaussian blur an image volume", {
  gmask <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask2.nii.gz", package="neuroim2")
  gmask <- read_vol(gmask)
  g1 <- neuroim2::gaussian_blur(gmask, gmask, sigma=2)
  g2 <- neuroim2::gaussian_blur(gmask, gmask, sigma=8)
  g3 <- neuroim2::gaussian_blur(gmask, gmask, sigma=8, window=3)

  expect_true(!is.null(g1))
  expect_true(!is.null(g2))
  expect_true(!is.null(g3))

})

test_that("can apply guided filter image volume", {
  gmask <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask2.nii.gz", package="neuroim2")
  gmask <- read_vol(gmask)
  g1 <- neuroim2::guided_filter(gmask, 4)

  expect_true(!is.null(g1))

})

  fac <- as.factor(c(1, 1, 2, 2))

  # Define custom function
  custom_fun <- function(x) sum(x)

  # Test split_reduce
  output <- split_reduce(mat, fac, custom_fun)

  # Expected output
  expected_output <- matrix(c(3, 7, 11, 15,19,23), nrow=2)

  expect_equivalent(output, expected_output)
})

# Test cases for split_scale
test_that("split_scale works with matrix and factor inputs", {
  # Create input matrix
  mat <- matrix(1:12, nrow=4)

  # Split matrix by factor
  fac <- as.factor(c(1, 1, 2, 2))

  # Test split_scale
  output <- split_scale(mat, fac, center=TRUE, scale=TRUE)

  # Expected output
  expected_output <- rbind(base::scale(mat[1:2,], center=TRUE, scale=TRUE),
                           base::scale(mat[3:4,], center=TRUE, scale=TRUE))

  expect_equivalent(output, expected_output)
})

library(purrr)
library(testthat)
library(assertthat)

gmask5 <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask_v4.nii", package="neuroim2")
mask <- system.file("extdata", "global_mask2.nii.gz", package="neuroim2")

context("neurovecseq")

test_that("can construct a NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  expect_equal(dim(cvec), dim(vs))
  expect_equal(space(cvec), space(vs))

})

test_that("can linearly index a NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  expect_equal(vs[1], cvec[1])
  for (i in 1:20) {
    idx <- sample(1:prod(dim(cvec)), 50)
    expect_equal(vs[idx], cvec[idx])
  }
})

test_that("can array index a NeuroVecSeq", {
  vec <- read_vec(gmask5)
  cvec <- concat(vec,vec,vec)

  vs <- NeuroVecSeq(vec,vec,vec)
  expect_equal(vs[1], cvec[1])

  expect_equal(vs[1,,,], cvec[1,,,])
  expect_equal(vs[1,2,,], cvec[1,2,,])
  expect_equal(vs[1,2,3,], cvec[1,2,3,])
  expect_equal(vs[1,2,3,4], cvec[1,2,3,4])
  expect_equal(vs[1:2,2,3,4], cvec[1:2,2,3,4])
  expect_equal(vs[1:2,2,3,3:4], cvec[1:2,2,3,3:4])
  expect_equal(vs[1:2,2,,3:4], cvec[1:2,2,,3:4])
  expect_equal(vs[1:2,,,3:4], cvec[1:2,,,3:4])
  expect_equal(vs[,,,3:4], cvec[,,,3:4])

})

"""Tests for Phase 7: Spatial Filtering.

Direct translation of R's neuroim2 tests for spatial filtering functionality.
"""

import pytest
import numpy as np
from scipy import ndimage

from pyneuroim import (
    # Core classes
    DenseNeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol, DenseNeuroVec, NeuroSpace,
    # Kernel classes
    Kernel, gaussian_kernel, spherical_kernel, box_kernel, embed_kernel,
    # Filtering functions
    gaussian_blur, guided_filter, bilateral_filter, bilateral_filter_vec
)


class TestKernel:
    """Test Kernel class functionality."""
    
    def test_kernel_creation(self):
        """Test basic kernel creation."""
        width = (3, 3, 3)
        weights = np.ones(27) / 27  # Uniform weights
        voxels = np.array([[i, j, k] for i in [-1, 0, 1] 
                          for j in [-1, 0, 1] 
                          for k in [-1, 0, 1]])
        coords = voxels.astype(float)
        
        kernel = Kernel(width, weights, voxels, coords)
        
        assert kernel.width == (3, 3, 3)
        assert len(kernel.weights) == 27
        assert kernel.voxels.shape == (27, 3)
        assert kernel.coords.shape == (27, 3)
    
    def test_kernel_get_voxels(self):
        """Test getting voxel locations with offset."""
        width = (3, 3, 3)
        weights = np.ones(27) / 27
        voxels = np.array([[i, j, k] for i in [-1, 0, 1] 
                          for j in [-1, 0, 1]

        coords = voxels.astype(float)
        
        kernel = Kernel(width, weights, voxels, coords)
        
        # Without center

"""Tests for statistical operations."""

import pytest
import numpy as np
from pyneuroim import (
    NeuroSpace, DenseNeuroVol, DenseNeuroVec, SparseNeuroVol,
    LogicalNeuroVol, ClusteredNeuroVol, ROIVol,
    split_blocks, split_clusters, split_fill, split_reduce,
    split_scale, partition, map_values, centroids
)


class TestSplitBlocks:
    """Test split_blocks function."""
    
    def setup_method(self):
        """Set up test data."""
        self.space = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10])
        
        # Create test volume with known patterns
        self.vol_data = np.zeros((10, 10, 10))
        # Fill specific locations that we'll index
        flat_data = self.vol_data.ravel(order='F')
        flat_data[0:3] = 1.0  # First 3 indices
        flat_data[500:503] = 2.0  # Indices 500-502
        self.vol_data = flat_data.reshape((10, 10, 10), order='F')
        self.vol = DenseNeuroVol(self.vol_data, self.space)
        
        # Create indices and block IDs
        self.indices = np.array([0, 1, 2, 500, 501, 502])  # Mix of blocks
        self.block_ids = np.array([1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 2])
    
    def test_split_blocks_volume(self):
        """Test splitting a volume into blocks."""
        blocks = split_blocks(self.vol, self.indices, self.block_ids)
        
        assert len(blocks) == 2
        
        # Check each block
        for i, block in enumerate(blocks):
            assert isinstance(block, SparseNeuroVol)
            assert block.space == self.space
            
            if i == 0:  # Block 1
                assert len(block.data) == 3
                assert np.all(block.data == 1.0)
            else:  # Block 2
                assert len(block.data) == 3
                assert np.all(block.data == 2.0)

"""Comprehensive tests for NeuroVec classes based on R neuroim2 tests."""

import pytest
import numpy as np
import tempfile
import os
from pyneuroim import (
    NeuroSpace, DenseNeuroVec, SparseNeuroVec, NeuroVec,
    DenseNeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol, neurovol, neurovec, neurovecseq,
    BigNeuroVec, FileBackedNeuroVec, MappedNeuroVec
)


def gen_dat(d1=12, d2=12, d3=12, d4=4, rand=False):
    """Generate test NeuroVec data (matches R helper function)."""
    if rand:
        dat = np.random.randn(d1, d2, d3, d4)
    else:
        dat = np.zeros((d1, d2, d3, d4))
    spc = NeuroSpace(dim=[d1, d2, d3, d4])
    return DenseNeuroVec(dat, spc)


class TestNeuroVecConstruction:
    """Test NeuroVec construction from various data sources."""
    
    def test_construct_dense_neurovec(self):
        """Test basic DenseNeuroVec construction."""
        bv = gen_dat(12, 12, 12, 4)
        assert bv is not None
        assert bv[0, 0, 0, 0] == 0
        assert bv[11, 11, 11, 3] == 0
        assert bv.shape == (12, 12, 12, 4)
    
    def test_construct_from_matrix_time_x_voxels(self):
        """Test construction from matrix (time x voxels)."""
        spc = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10, 4])
        mat = np.random.randn(4, 1000)  # time x voxels
        vec = neurovec(mat, spc)
        
        assert vec.shape == (10, 10, 10, 4)
        assert isinstance(vec, DenseNeuroVec)
    
    def test_construct_from_matrix_voxels_x_time(self):
        """Test construction from matrix (voxels x time)."""
        spc = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10, 4])
        mat = np.random.randn(1000, 4)  # voxels x time
        vec = neurovec(mat.T, spc)  # Transpose to time x voxels
        
        assert vec.shape == (10, 10, 10, 4)

# R to Python Test Mapping

This document shows which R tests from neuroim2 have corresponding Python tests in pyneuroim.

## Test Coverage Summary

### ✅ Tests with Python Equivalents

1. **test-neurovol.R** → Multiple Python test files:
   - `test_neurovol.py`
   - `test_neurovol_comprehensive.py`
   - `test_neurovol_edge_cases.py`
   - `test_phase2_neurovol.py`
   - `test_phase2_neurovol_core.py`
   - `test_dense_neuro_vol.py`

2. **test-neurovec.R** → Multiple Python test files:
   - `test_neurovec_comprehensive.py`
   - `test_neurovec_edge_cases.py`
   - `test_phase3_neurovec.py`
   - `test_phase3_neurovec_core.py`

3. **test-neuroslice.R** → Python test files:
   - `test_phase4_neuroslice.py`

4. **test-conn_comp.R** → Python test files:
   - `test_connected_components.py`

5. **test-roivol.R** → Multiple Python test files:
   - `test_roi_comprehensive.py`
   - `test_phase5_roi.py`
   - `test_series_roi.py`
   - `test_series_roi_memmapped.py`

6. **test-resample.R** → Python test files:
   - `test_phase8_resample.py`

7. **test-searchlight.R** → Multiple Python test files:
   - `test_searchlight.py`
   - `test_searchlight_parallel.py`

8. **test-spatfilter.R** → Python test files:
   - `test_phase7_spatial_filters.py` (includes gaussian_blur, guided_filter, bilateral_filter)

9. **test-hypervec.R** → Multiple Python test files:
   - `test_neurohypervec.py`
   - `test_neuro_hypervec.py`

10. **test-filebacked.R** → Python test files:
    - `test_file_backed_neuro_vec.py`

11. **test-mapped.R** → Python test files:
    - `test_memory_mapped.py`

12. **test-latentvec.R** → Python test files:
    - `test_latent_neuro_vec.py`

13. **test-vignette-neuro-vectors.R** → Python test files:
    - `test_vignette_neuro_vectors.py`

14. **test-vignette-pipelines.R** → Python test files:
    - `test_vignette_pipelines.py`

15. **test-vignette-roi.R** → Python test files:
    - `test_vignette_roi.py`

16. **test-splitscale.R** → Python test files:
    - `test_stats.py` (includes split_reduce, split_scale)
    - `test_stats_extra.py`

### ⚠️ Tests with Partial or Different Implementation

1. **test-clusteredneurovol.R** → Partial coverage:
   - ClusteredNeuroVol functionality appears in `test_stats.py` and `test_connected_components.py`
   - Not a dedicated test file but functionality is tested

import numpy as np
from typing import List, Dict
from .neuro_vol import NeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol, SparseNeuroVol, DenseNeuroVol
from .neuro_space import NeuroSpace

class ClusteredNeuroVol(NeuroVol):
    """
    A class representing a clustered volumetric neuroimaging data.
    """

    def __init__(self, mask: LogicalNeuroVol, clusters: np.ndarray, label_map: Dict[str, int] = None):
        """
        Initialize a ClusteredNeuroVol.

        :param mask: A LogicalNeuroVol representing the spatial domain of the clusters.
        :param clusters: An array of cluster labels for each voxel in the mask.
        :param label_map: A dictionary mapping cluster names to cluster numbers.
        """
        super().__init__(mask.space)
        self.mask = mask
        self.clusters = clusters
        self.label_map = label_map or {}
        self.cluster_map = self._create_cluster_map()

    def _create_cluster_map(self) -> Dict[int, np.ndarray]:
        """
        Create a mapping from cluster IDs to the set of 1D spatial indices belonging to that cluster.
        """
        unique_clusters = np.unique(self.clusters)
        return {cluster: np.where(self.clusters == cluster)[0] for cluster in unique_clusters}

    def __getitem__(self, key):
        if isinstance(key, str):
            # If key is a string, assume it's a cluster label
            cluster_id = self.label_map.get(key)
            if cluster_id is None:
                raise KeyError(f"Cluster label '{key}' not found in label map")
            return self.cluster_map[cluster_id]
        elif isinstance(key, int):
            # If key is an integer, assume it's a cluster ID
            return self.cluster_map.get(key, np.array([]))
        else:
            # Otherwise, delegate to the mask's __getitem__
            return self.mask[key]

    def __setitem__(self, key, value):
        raise NotImplementedError("ClusteredNeuroVol does not support item assignment")

    def get_cluster_mask(self, cluster_id: int) -> LogicalNeuroVol:
        """
        Get a binary mask for a specific cluster.

        :param cluster_id: The ID of the cluster.
        :return: A LogicalNeuroVol representing the mask of the specified cluster.
        """
        cluster_mask = np.zeros(self.shape, dtype=bool)
        cluster_mask[np.unravel_index(self.cluster_map[cluster_id], self.shape)] = True
        return LogicalNeuroVol(cluster_mask, self.space)

    def get_cluster_data(self, data: NeuroVol, cluster_id: int) -> np.ndarray:
        """
        Get the data for a specific cluster from a NeuroVol.

        :param data: A NeuroVol containing the data to extract.
        :param cluster_id: The ID of the cluster.
        :return: An array of data values for the specified cluster.
        """
        if not isinstance(data, NeuroVol) or data.space != self.space:
            raise ValueError("Data must be a NeuroVol with matching space")
        return data[np.unravel_index(self.cluster_map[cluster_id], self.shape)]

    def to_sparse(self) -> SparseNeuroVol:
        """
        Convert the clustered volume to a SparseNeuroVol.

        :return: A SparseNeuroVol representation of the clustered volume.
        """
        return SparseNeuroVol(self.clusters[self.mask.data], self.space, np.where(self.mask.data)[0])

    def cluster_sizes(self) -> Dict[int, int]:
        """
        Get the size (number of voxels) of each cluster.

        :return: A dictionary mapping cluster IDs to their sizes.
        """
        return {cluster_id: len(indices) for cluster_id, indices in self.cluster_map.items()}

    def num_clusters(self) -> int:
        """
        Get the number of clusters.

        :return: The number of unique clusters.
        """
        return len(self.cluster_map)

    def cluster_centers(self) -> Dict[int, np.ndarray]:
        """
        Calculate the center of mass for each cluster.

        :return: A dictionary mapping cluster IDs to their center coordinates.
        """
        centers = {}
        for cluster_id, indices in self.cluster_map.items():
            coords = np.array(np.unravel_index(indices, self.shape)).T
            center = np.mean(coords, axis=0)
            centers[cluster_id] = center
        return centers

    @property
    def values(self) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get the cluster label values."""
        return self.clusters

    def as_sparse(self) -> SparseNeuroVol:
        """Convert to SparseNeuroVol."""
        return self.to_sparse()
    
    def as_dense(self) -> DenseNeuroVol:
        """Convert to DenseNeuroVol."""
        # Create dense representation
        dense_data = np.zeros(self.shape, dtype=self.clusters.dtype)
        mask_indices = np.where(self.mask.data.ravel())[0]
        dense_data.ravel()[mask_indices] = self.clusters
        return DenseNeuroVol(dense_data, self.space)
    
    def as_logical(self) -> LogicalNeuroVol:
        """Convert to LogicalNeuroVol."""
        return self.mask
    
    def _arithmetic_op(self, other, op):
        """Arithmetic operations not supported for ClusteredNeuroVol."""
        raise NotImplementedError("Arithmetic operations not supported for ClusteredNeuroVol")
    
    def _comparison_op(self, other, op):
        """Comparison operations not supported for ClusteredNeuroVol."""
        raise NotImplementedError("Comparison operations not supported for ClusteredNeuroVol")

    def __repr__(self):
        return (f"ClusteredNeuroVol(\n"
                f"  Num clusters   : {self.num_clusters()}\n"
                f"  Dimension      : {self.shape}\n"
                f"  Spacing        : {' X '.join(map(str, self.space.spacing))}\n"
                f"  Origin         : {' X '.join(map(str, self.space.origin))}\n"
                f"  Axes           : {' '.join(ax.axis for ax in self.space.axes)}")

def clustered_neuro_vol(mask: LogicalNeuroVol, clusters: np.ndarray, label_map: Dict[str, int] = None) -> ClusteredNeuroVol:
    """
    Factory function to create a ClusteredNeuroVol instance.

    :param mask: A LogicalNeuroVol representing the spatial domain of the clusters.
    :param clusters: An array of cluster labels for each voxel in the mask.
    :param label_map: A dictionary mapping cluster names to cluster numbers.
    :return: A new ClusteredNeuroVol instance.
    """
    return ClusteredNeuroVol(mask, clusters, label_map)

content.content:
1→"""Spatial filtering functions for neuroimaging data.
     2→
     3→Direct translation of R's neuroim2 spatial filtering functions.
     4→"""
     5→
     6→import numpy as np
     7→from scipy import ndimage
     8→from typing import Union, Optional
     9→from .neuro_vol import NeuroVol, DenseNeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol
    10→from .neuro_vec import NeuroVec, DenseNeuroVec
    11→from .kernel import Kernel, gaussian_kernel
    12→
    13→def gaussian_blur(vol: NeuroVol, mask: Optional[LogicalNeuroVol] = None, 
    14→                 sigma: float = 2, window: int = 1) -> DenseNeuroVol:
    15→    """Apply Gaussian blur to a volumetric image.
    16→    
    17→    This function applies an isotropic discrete Gaussian kernel to smooth
    18→    a volumetric image (3D brain MRI data). The blurring is performed within
    19→    a specified image mask, with customizable kernel parameters.
    20→    
    21→    Parameters
    22→    ----------
    23→    vol : NeuroVol
    24→        The image volume to be smoothed
    25→    mask : LogicalNeuroVol, optional
    26→        Image mask defining the region where blurring is applied.
    27→        If not provided, the entire volume is processed.
    28→    sigma : float
    29→        Standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel (default is 2)
    30→    window : int
    31→        Number of voxels to include on each side of the center voxel.
    32→        For example, window=1 results in a 3x3x3 kernel (default is 1)
    33→        
    34→    Returns
    35→    -------
    36→    DenseNeuroVol
    37→        The smoothed image
    38→        
    39→    R Equivalent
    40→    ------------
    41→    neuroim2::gaussian_blur
    42→    """
    43→    if window < 1:
    44→        raise ValueError("Window size must be at least 1")
    45→    if sigma <= 0:
    46→        raise ValueError("Sigma must be positive")
    47→
    48→    # Work with the original data when possible
    49→    data = vol.data
    50→    
    51→    if mask is not None:
    52→        # Create output array only when mask is provided
    53→        output_data = data.copy()
    54→        mask_indices = np.where(mask.data)
    55→        # Apply gaussian filter only to masked region for efficiency
    56→        blurred_data = ndimage.gaussian_filter(data, sigma=sigma, truncate=window)
    57→        output_data[mask_indices] = blurred_data[mask_indices]
    58→    else:
    59→        # When no mask, directly apply filter (no copy needed)
    60→        output_data = ndimage.gaussian_filter(data, sigma=sigma, truncate=window)
    61→
    62→    return DenseNeuroVol(output_data, vol.space)
    63→
    64→def box_blur(data: np.ndarray, mask_indices: np.ndarray, radius: int) -> np.ndarray:
    65→    """Helper function for guided filter: applies box blur to the data."""
    66→    kernel = np.ones((2*radius+1, 2*radius+1, 2*radius+1)) / ((2*radius+1)**3)
    67→    blurred = ndimage.convolve(data, kernel, mode='constant', cval=0.0)
    68→    return blurred[mask_indices]
    69→
    70→def guided_filter(vol: NeuroVol, radius: int = 4, epsilon: float = 0.49) -> DenseNeuroVol:
    71→    """Apply edge-preserving guided filter to a volumetric image.
    72→    
    73→    This function applies a guided filter to perform edge-preserving smoothing.
    74→    The guided filter smooths the image while preserving edges, providing a
    75→    balance between noise reduction and structural preservation.
    76→    
    77→    Parameters
    78→    ----------
    79→    vol : NeuroVol
    80→        The image volume to be filtered
    81→    radius : int
    82→        Spatial radius of the filter (default is 4)
    83→    epsilon : float
    84→        Regularization parameter controlling the degree of smoothing
    85→        and edge preservation (default is 0.49)
    86→        
    87→    Returns
    88→    -------
    89→    DenseNeuroVol
    90→        The filtered image
    91→        
    92→    R Equivalent
    93→    ------------
    94→    neuroim2::guided_filter
    95→    """
    96→    if radius < 1:
    97→        raise ValueError("Radius must be at least 1")
    98→    if epsilon < 0:
    99→        raise ValueError("Epsilon must be non-negative")
   100→    
   101→    data = vol.data
   102→    mask_indices = np.where(data != 0)
   103→
   104→    # Apply box blur to compute local means
   105→    mean_I = box_blur(vol, mask_indices, radius)
   106→    
   107→    # Create temporary volumes for squared data
   108→    vol_squared = DenseNeuroVol(data * data, vol.space)
   109→    mean_II = box_blur(vol_squared, mask_indices, radius)
   110→
   111→    var_I = mean_II - mean_I * mean_I
   112→    mean_p = mean_I
   113→    mean_Ip = mean_II
   114→
   115→    cov_Ip = mean_Ip - mean_I * mean_p
   116→    a = cov_Ip / (var_I + epsilon)
   117→    b = mean_p - a * mean_I
   118→
   119→    # Create temporary volumes for a and b
   120→    a_full = np.zeros_like(data)
   121→    b_full = np.zeros_like(data)
   122→    a_full[mask_indices] = a
   123→    b_full[mask_indices] = b
   124→    
   125→    a_vol = DenseNeuroVol(a_full, vol.space)
   126→    b_vol = DenseNeuroVol(b_full, vol.space)
   127→    
   128→    mean_a = box_blur(a_vol, mask_indices, radius)
   129→    mean_b = box_blur(b_vol, mask_indices, radius)
   130→
   131→    # Apply the guided filter formula
   132→    output_data = data.copy()
   133→    output_data[mask_indices] = mean_a * data[mask_indices] + mean_b
   134→
   135→    return DenseNeuroVol(output_data, vol.space)
   136→
   137→def bilateral_filter(vol: NeuroVol, mask: Optional[LogicalNeuroVol] = None, 
   138→                     spatial_sigma: float = 2, intensity_sigma: float = 1, 
   139→                     window: int = 1) -> DenseNeuroVol:
   140→    """Apply bilateral filter to a volumetric image.
   141→    
   142→    This function smooths a volumetric image using a bilateral filter.
   143→    The bilateral filter considers both spatial closeness and intensity
   144→    similarity for smoothing.
   145→    
   146→    Parameters
   147→    ----------
   148→    vol : NeuroVol
   149→        The image volume to be smoothed
   150→    mask : LogicalNeuroVol, optional
   151→        Image mask defining the region where filtering is applied.
   152→        If not provided, the entire volume is considered.
   153→    spatial_sigma : float
   154→        Standard deviation of the spatial Gaussian kernel (default is 2)
   155→    intensity_sigma : float
   156→        Standard deviation of the intensity Gaussian kernel (default is 1)
   157→    window : int
   158→        Number of voxels around the center voxel to include on each side.
   159→        For example, window=1 for a 3x3x3 kernel (default is 1)
   160→        
   161→    Returns
   162→    -------
   163→    DenseNeuroVol
   164→        The smoothed image
   165→        
   166→    R Equivalent
   167→    ------------
   168→    neuroim2::bilateral_filter
   169→    """
   170→    if window < 1:
   171→        raise ValueError("Window size must be at least 1")
   172→    if spatial_sigma <= 0 or intensity_sigma <= 0:
   173→        raise ValueError("Sigma values must be positive")
   174→
   175→    data = vol.data
   176→    if mask is not None:
   177→        mask_indices = np.where(mask.data)
   178→    else:
   179→        mask_indices = np.where(np.ones_like(data, dtype=bool))
   180→
   181→    # Create spatial and intensity kernels
   182→    spatial_kernel = np.exp(-np.arange(-window, window+1)**2 / (2 * spatial_sigma**2))
   183→    spatial_kernel = spatial_kernel[:, np.newaxis, np.newaxis] * spatial_kernel[np.newaxis, :, np.newaxis] * spatial_kernel[np.newaxis, np.newaxis, :]
   184→
   185→    def bilateral_at_point(point):
   186→        i, j, k = point
   187→        local_region = data[max(0, i-window):i+window+1, 
   188→                            max(0, j-window):j+window+1, 
   189→                            max(0, k-window):k+window+1]
   190→        
   191→        intensity_diff = local_region - data[i, j, k]
   192→        intensity_kernel = np.exp(-intensity_diff**2 / (2 * intensity_sigma**2))
   193→

   195→

   197→        normalization = np.sum(kernel)
   198→        
   199→        return weighted_sum / normalization
   200→
   201→    # Initialize output - only copy if we have a mask
   202→    if mask is not None:
   203→        output_data = data.copy()
   204→        for point in zip(*mask_indices):
   205→            output_data[point] = bilateral_at_point(point)

   207→        # Process entire volume

   212→                    output_data[i, j, k] = bilateral_at_point((i, j, k))

   214→    return DenseNeuroVol(output_data, vol.space)

   216→def bilateral_filter_vec(vec: NeuroVec, mask: Optional[LogicalNeuroVol] = None, 
   217→                         spatial_sigma: float = 2, intensity_sigma: float = 1, 
   218→                         window: int = 1) -> DenseNeuroVec:

   259→def box_blur(vol: NeuroVol, mask_indices: tuple, radius: int) -> np.ndarray:
   260→    """Helper function for guided filter: applies box blur to the data.

"""Spatial filtering functions for neuroimaging data.

Direct translation of R's neuroim2 spatial filtering functions.
"""

import numpy as np
from scipy import ndimage
from typing import Union, Optional
from .neuro_vol import NeuroVol, DenseNeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol
from .neuro_vec import NeuroVec, DenseNeuroVec
from .kernel import Kernel, gaussian_kernel

def gaussian_blur(vol: NeuroVol, mask: Optional[LogicalNeuroVol] = None, 
                 sigma: float = 2, window: int = 1) -> DenseNeuroVol:
    """Apply Gaussian blur to a volumetric image.
    
    This function applies an isotropic discrete Gaussian kernel to smooth
    a volumetric image (3D brain MRI data). The blurring is performed within
    a specified image mask, with customizable kernel parameters.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    vol : NeuroVol
        The image volume to be smoothed
    mask : LogicalNeuroVol, optional
        Image mask defining the region where blurring is applied.
        If not provided, the entire volume is processed.
    sigma : float
        Standard deviation of the Gaussian kernel (default is 2)
    window : int
        Number of voxels to include on each side of the center voxel.
        For example, window=1 results in a 3x3x3 kernel (default is 1)
        
    Returns
    -------
    DenseNeuroVol
        The smoothed image
        
    R Equivalent
    ------------
    neuroim2::gaussian_blur
    """
    if window < 1:
        raise ValueError("Window size must be at least 1")
    if sigma <= 0:
        raise ValueError("Sigma must be positive")

    # Work with the original data when possible
    data = vol.data
    
    if mask is not None:
        # Create output array only when mask is provided
        output_data = data.copy()
        mask_indices = np.where(mask.data)
        # Apply gaussian filter only to masked region for efficiency
        blurred_data = ndimage.gaussian_filter(data, sigma=sigma, truncate=window)
        output_data[mask_indices] = blurred_data[mask_indices]
    else:
        # When no mask, directly apply filter (no copy needed)
        output_data = ndimage.gaussian_filter(data, sigma=sigma, truncate=window)

    return DenseNeuroVol(output_data, vol.space)

def box_blur(data: np.ndarray, mask_indices: np.ndarray, radius: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """Helper function for guided filter: applies box blur to the data."""
    kernel = np.ones((2*radius+1, 2*radius+1, 2*radius+1)) / ((2*radius+1)**3)
    blurred = ndimage.convolve(data, kernel, mode='constant', cval=0.0)
    return blurred[mask_indices]

def guided_filter(vol: NeuroVol, radius: int = 4, epsilon: float = 0.49) -> DenseNeuroVol:
    """Apply edge-preserving guided filter to a volumetric image.
    
    This function applies a guided filter to perform edge-preserving smoothing.
    The guided filter smooths the image while preserving edges, providing a
    balance between noise reduction and structural preservation.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    vol : NeuroVol
        The image volume to be filtered
    radius : int
        Spatial radius of the filter (default is 4)
    epsilon : float
        Regularization parameter controlling the degree of smoothing
        and edge preservation (default is 0.49)
        
    Returns
    -------
    DenseNeuroVol
        The filtered image
        
    R Equivalent
    ------------
    neuroim2::guided_filter
    """
    if radius < 1:
        raise ValueError("Radius must be at least 1")
    if epsilon < 0:
        raise ValueError("Epsilon must be non-negative")
    
    data = vol.data
    mask_indices = np.where(data != 0)

    # Apply box blur to compute local means
    mean_I = box_blur(vol, mask_indices, radius)
    
    # Create temporary volumes for squared data
    vol_squared = DenseNeuroVol(data * data, vol.space)
    mean_II = box_blur(vol_squared, mask_indices, radius)

    var_I = mean_II - mean_I * mean_I
    mean_p = mean_I
    mean_Ip = mean_II

    cov_Ip = mean_Ip - mean_I * mean_p
    a = cov_Ip / (var_I + epsilon)
    b = mean_p - a * mean_I

    # Create temporary volumes for a and b
    a_full = np.zeros_like(data)
    b_full = np.zeros_like(data)
    a_full[mask_indices] = a
    b_full[mask_indices] = b
    
    a_vol = DenseNeuroVol(a_full, vol.space)
    b_vol = DenseNeuroVol(b_full, vol.space)
    
    mean_a = box_blur(a_vol, mask_indices, radius)
    mean_b = box_blur(b_vol, mask_indices, radius)

    # Apply the guided filter formula
    output_data = data.copy()
    output_data[mask_indices] = mean_a * data[mask_indices] + mean_b

    return DenseNeuroVol(output_data, vol.space)

def bilateral_filter(vol: NeuroVol, mask: Optional[LogicalNeuroVol] = None, 
                     spatial_sigma: float = 2, intensity_sigma: float = 1, 
                     window: int = 1) -> DenseNeuroVol:
    """Apply bilateral filter to a volumetric image.
    
    This function smooths a volumetric image using a bilateral filter.
    The bilateral filter considers both spatial closeness and intensity
    similarity for smoothing.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    vol : NeuroVol
        The image volume to be smoothed
    mask : LogicalNeuroVol, optional
        Image mask defining the region where filtering is applied.
        If not provided, the entire volume is considered.
    spatial_sigma : float
        Standard deviation of the spatial Gaussian kernel (default is 2)
    intensity_sigma : float
        Standard deviation of the intensity Gaussian kernel (default is 1)
    window : int
        Number of voxels around the center voxel to include on each side.
        For example, window=1 for a 3x3x3 kernel (default is 1)
        
    Returns
    -------
    DenseNeuroVol
        The smoothed image
        
    R Equivalent
    ------------
    neuroim2::bilateral_filter
    """
    if window < 1:
        raise ValueError("Window size must be at least 1")
    if spatial_sigma <= 0 or intensity_sigma <= 0:
        raise ValueError("Sigma values must be positive")

    data = vol.data
    if mask is not None:
        mask_indices = np.where(mask.data)
    else:
        mask_indices = np.where(np.ones_like(data, dtype=bool))

    # Create spatial and intensity kernels
    spatial_kernel = np.exp(-np.arange(-window, window+1)**2 / (2 * spatial_sigma**2))
    spatial_kernel = spatial_kernel[:, np.newaxis, np.newaxis] * spatial_kernel[np.newaxis, :, np.newaxis] * spatial_kernel[np.newaxis, np.newaxis, :]

    def bilateral_at_point(point):
        i, j, k = point
        local_region = data[max(0, i-window):i+window+1, 
                            max(0, j-window):j+window+1, 
                            max(0, k-window):k+window+1]
        
        intensity_diff = local_region - data[i, j, k]
        intensity_kernel = np.exp(-intensity_diff**2 / (2 * intensity_sigma**2))
        
        kernel = spatial_kernel[:local_region.shape[0], :local_region.shape[1], :local_region.shape[2]] * intensity_kernel
        
        weighted_sum = np.sum(local_region * kernel)
        normalization = np.sum(kernel)
        
        return weighted_sum / normalization

    # Initialize output - only copy if we have a mask
    if mask is not None:
        output_data = data.copy()
        for point in zip(*mask_indices):
            output_data[point] = bilateral_at_point(point)
    else:
        # Process entire volume
        output_data = np.empty_like(data)
        for i in range(data.shape[0]):
            for j in range(data.shape[1]):
                for k in range(data.shape[2]):
                    output_data[i, j, k] = bilateral_at_point((i, j, k))

    return DenseNeuroVol(output_data, vol.space)

def bilateral_filter_vec(vec: NeuroVec, mask: Optional[LogicalNeuroVol] = None, 
                         spatial_sigma: float = 2, intensity_sigma: float = 1, 
                         window: int = 1) -> DenseNeuroVec:
    """Apply bilateral filter to each volume of a NeuroVec.
    
    This function applies a bilateral filter to each volume of a NeuroVec object.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    vec : NeuroVec
        The volumes to be filtered
    mask : LogicalNeuroVol, optional
        Binary mask specifying the region of interest.
        If not provided, the whole volume is considered.
    spatial_sigma : float
        Spatial sigma for the bilateral filter (default is 2)
    intensity_sigma : float
        Intensity sigma for the bilateral filter (default is 1)
    window : int
        Size of the window for the bilateral filter (default is 1)
        
    Returns
    -------
    DenseNeuroVec
        The filtered volumes
        
    R Equivalent
    ------------
    neuroim2::bilateral_filter_vec
    """
    # Extract individual volumes and filter
    filtered_vols = []
    for i in range(vec.shape[3]):
        vol = vec.vols(i)
        filtered_vol = bilateral_filter(vol, mask, spatial_sigma, intensity_sigma, window)
        filtered_vols.append(filtered_vol)
    
    # Stack filtered volumes
    filtered_data = np.stack([vol.data for vol in filtered_vols], axis=-1)
    return DenseNeuroVec(filtered_data, vec.space)



def box_blur(vol: NeuroVol, mask_indices: tuple, radius: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """Helper function for guided filter: applies box blur to the data.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    vol : NeuroVol
        Volume to blur
    mask_indices : tuple
        Indices where to apply the blur
    radius : int
        Radius of the box filter
        
    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        Blurred values at mask indices
        
    Notes
    -----
    This is used internally by guided_filter.
    """
    data = vol.data
    kernel_size = 2 * radius + 1
    kernel = np.ones((kernel_size, kernel_size, kernel_size)) / (kernel_size ** 3)
    
    # Apply convolution
    blurred = ndimage.convolve(data, kernel, mode='constant', cval=0.0)
    
    # Return values at mask indices
    return blurred[mask_indices]

1→"""Statistical operations for neuroimaging data.
     2→
     3→This module provides functions for splitting, partitioning, and
     4→analyzing neuroimaging data using various statistical approaches.
     5→
     6→Direct translation of R's neuroim2 statistical functions.
     7→"""
     8→
     9→import numpy as np
    10→from typing import List, Tuple, Dict, Callable, Optional, Union
    11→from .neuro_vol import NeuroVol, DenseNeuroVol, SparseNeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol
    12→from .neuro_vec import NeuroVec, DenseNeuroVec
    13→from .neuro_space import NeuroSpace
    14→from .roi import ROIVol
    15→from .clustered_neuro_vol import ClusteredNeuroVol
    16→
    17→
    18→def split_blocks(x: Union[NeuroVol, NeuroVec], indices: np.ndarray, 
    19→                 block_ids: np.ndarray) -> List[Union[NeuroVol, NeuroVec]]:
    20→    """Split a NeuroVol or NeuroVec into blocks using indices.
    21→    
    22→    Creates separate volumes/vectors for each unique block ID, where each
    23→    block contains only the data from voxels with that block ID.
    24→    
    25→    Parameters
    26→    ----------
    27→    x : NeuroVol or NeuroVec
    28→        The data to split
    29→    indices : np.ndarray
    30→        1D indices into the data array
    31→    block_ids : np.ndarray
    32→        Block ID for each index (same length as indices)
    33→        
    34→    Returns
    35→    -------
    36→    list of NeuroVol or NeuroVec
    37→        List of blocks, one for each unique block ID
    38→        
    39→    R Equivalent
    40→    ------------
    41→    neuroim2::split_blocks
    42→    """
    43→    if len(indices) != len(block_ids):
    44→        raise ValueError("indices and block_ids must have same length")
    45→    
    46→    unique_blocks = np.unique(block_ids)
    47→    blocks = []
    48→    
    49→    for block_id in unique_blocks:
    50→        # Get indices for this block
    51→        block_mask = block_ids == block_id
    52→        block_indices = indices[block_mask]
    53→        
    54→        if isinstance(x, NeuroVec):
    55→            # Extract data for this block
    56→            from .neuro_vec import SparseNeuroVec
    57→            
    58→            # Need to reshape x.data to 4D if it isn't already
    59→            if x.data.ndim == 2:
    60→                data_4d = x.data.reshape(x.shape)
    61→            else:
    62→                data_4d = x.data
    63→            
    64→            # Create array to hold time series for this block
    65→            # Time is last dimension, so shape[-1] is n_timepoints
    66→            block_series = np.zeros((len(block_indices), x.shape[-1]))  # (n_voxels, n_timepoints)
    67→            
    68→            for i, idx in enumerate(block_indices):
    69→                # Convert linear index to grid coordinates (spatial dimensions)
    70→                coords = np.unravel_index(idx, x.shape[:3], order='F')
    71→                # Extract time series for this voxel (time is last)
    72→                block_series[i, :] = data_4d[coords[0], coords[1], coords[2], :]
    73→            
    74→            # Create mask for this block
    75→            # For NeuroVec, time is always last dimension (x, y, z, time)
    76→            spatial_dims = x.space.dim[:3]
    77→            spatial_shape = x.shape[:3]
    78→                
    79→            mask_data = np.zeros(spatial_shape, dtype=bool)
    80→            mask_flat = mask_data.ravel(order='F')
    81→            mask_flat[block_indices] = True
    82→            mask_data = mask_flat.reshape(spatial_shape, order='F')
    83→            mask = LogicalNeuroVol(mask_data, NeuroSpace(dim=spatial_dims))
    84→            
    85→            # Create SparseNeuroVec for this block
    86→            # SparseNeuroVec expects data as (n_timepoints, n_voxels), so transpose
    87→            block_vec = SparseNeuroVec(
    88→                data=block_series.T,
    89→                space=x.space,
    90→                mask=mask
    91→            )
    92→            blocks.append(block_vec)
    93→            
    94→        else:  # NeuroVol
    95→            # Extract data for this block
    96→            # Use Fortran order for consistency with R
    97→            flat_data = x.data.ravel(order='F')
    98→            block_data = flat_data[block_indices]
    99→            
   100→            # Create SparseNeuroVol for this block
   101→            block_vol = SparseNeuroVol(
   102→                data=block_data,
   103→                space=x.space,
   104→                indices=block_indices
   105→            )
   106→            blocks.append(block_vol)
   107→    
   108→    return blocks
   109→
   110→
   111→def split_clusters(x: Union[NeuroVol, NeuroVec], 
   112→                   clusters: Union[ClusteredNeuroVol, NeuroVol]) -> List[ROIVol]:
   113→    """Split data into ROIs based on cluster labels.
   114→    
   115→    Creates a list of ROIVol objects, one for each cluster, containing
   116→    the data values from the input volume/vector.
   117→    
   118→    Parameters
   119→    ----------
   120→    x : NeuroVol or NeuroVec
   121→        The data to split
   122→    clusters : ClusteredNeuroVol or NeuroVol
   123→        Volume containing integer cluster labels
   124→        
   125→    Returns
   126→    -------
   127→    list of ROIVol
   128→        List of ROI volumes, one for each cluster
   129→        
   130→    R Equivalent
   131→    ------------
   132→    neuroim2::split_clusters
   133→    """
   134→    # For NeuroVec, compare spatial dimensions only
   135→    if isinstance(x, NeuroVec):
   136→        # Get spatial dimensions of NeuroVec (first 3 dimensions)
   137→        x_spatial_dim = x.space.dim[1:4] if x.shape[0] == x.space.dim[0] else x.space.dim[:3]
   138→        if not np.array_equal(x_spatial_dim, clusters.space.dim):
   139→            raise ValueError("x and clusters must have same spatial dimensions")
   140→    else:
   141→        if x.space != clusters.space:
   142→            raise ValueError("x and clusters must have same space")
   143→    
   144→    # Get cluster labels
   145→    if isinstance(clusters, ClusteredNeuroVol):
   146→        # Use the labeled data directly
   147→        unique_labels = np.unique(clusters.clusters)
   148→        cluster_data = clusters.as_dense().data
   149→    else:
   150→        # Assume it's a regular volume with integer labels
   151→        cluster_data = clusters.data
   152→        unique_labels = np.unique(cluster_data[cluster_data > 0])
   153→    
   154→    rois = []
   155→    
   156→    for label in unique_labels:
   157→        if label == 0:  # Skip background
   158→            continue
   159→            
   160→        # Get voxel coordinates for this cluster
   161→        mask = cluster_data == label
   162→        coords = np.column_stack(np.where(mask))
   163→        
   164→        # Extract data values
   165→        if isinstance(x, NeuroVec):
   166→            # For NeuroVec, extract time series for each voxel
   167→            # and average across voxels in the cluster
   168→            data_values = []
   169→            for coord in coords:
   170→                series = x[tuple(coord)]
   171→                data_values.append(np.mean(series))
   172→            data = np.array(data_values)
   173→        else:
   174→            # For NeuroVol, extract values directly
   175→            data = x.data[mask]
   176→        
   177→        # Create ROIVol
   178→        roi = ROIVol(data=data, space=x.space, coords=coords)
   179→        rois.append(roi)
   180→    
   181→    return rois
   182→
   183→
   184→def split_fill(x: NeuroVec, fac: np.ndarray) -> Dict[int, NeuroVec]:
   185→    """Split a NeuroVec by factor levels and fill a new NeuroVec.
   186→    
   187→    Splits the input NeuroVec according to levels of a factor, creating
   188→    a new NeuroVec for each level containing only the volumes from that level.
   189→    
   190→    Parameters
   191→    ----------
   192→    x : NeuroVec
   193→        The 4D data to split
   194→    fac : np.ndarray
   195→        Factor array with length equal to number of volumes
   196→        
   197→    Returns
   198→    -------
   199→    dict
   200→        Dictionary mapping factor levels to NeuroVec objects
   201→        
   202→    R Equivalent
   203→    ------------
   204→    neuroim2::split_fill
   205→    """
   206→    # Determine number of volumes (time dimension)
   207→    # For all NeuroVec types, time is last dimension (x, y, z, time)
   208→    n_volumes = x.shape[3]
   209→        
   210→    if len(fac) != n_volumes:
   211→        raise ValueError(f"Length of factor ({len(fac)}) must equal number of volumes ({n_volumes})")
   212→    
   213→    levels = np.unique(fac)
   214→    result = {}
   215→    
   216→    for level in levels:
   217→        # Get indices for this level
   218→        level_mask = fac == level
   219→        level_indices = np.where(level_mask)[0]
   220→        n_volumes = len(level_indices)
   221→        
   222→        # Extract subset of volumes
   223→        if isinstance(x, DenseNeuroVec):
   224→            # For dense, extract the relevant volumes
   225→            # When data is 4D, subset along first dimension (time)
   226→            subset_data = x.data[level_indices, :, :, :]
   227→            
   228→            # Create new space matching the subset shape
   229→            new_dim = list(subset_data.shape)  # This will be [n_volumes, x, y, z]
   230→            new_space = NeuroSpace(dim=new_dim, spacing=x.space.spacing, origin=x.space.origin,
   231→                                  trans=x.space.trans, axes=x.space.axes)
   232→            
   233→            level_vec = DenseNeuroVec(subset_data, new_space)
   234→        else:
   235→            # For sparse, need to handle carefully
   236→            from .neuro_vec import SparseNeuroVec
   237→            # Extract subset of time points
   238→            # SparseNeuroVec data is (time, voxels), so index first dimension
   239→            subset_data = x.data[level_indices, :]
   240→            
   241→            # Create new space with updated time dimension
   242→            new_dim = list(x.space.dim[:3]) + [n_volumes]
   243→            new_space = NeuroSpace(dim=new_dim, spacing=x.space.spacing, origin=x.space.origin,
   244→                                  trans=x.space.trans, axes=x.space.axes)
   245→            
   246→            level_vec = SparseNeuroVec(subset_data, new_space, x.mask)
   247→            
   248→        result[level] = level_vec
   249→    
   250→    return result
   251→
   252→
   253→def split_reduce(x: NeuroVec, fac: np.ndarray, 
   254→                 FUN: Callable[[np.ndarray], float]) -> NeuroVol:
   255→    """Reduce a NeuroVec by applying a function across factor levels.
   256→    
   257→    Splits the NeuroVec by factor levels and applies a reduction function
   258→    to each voxel's time series within each level, returning a volume
   259→    where each voxel contains the reduced value.
   260→    
   261→    Parameters
   262→    ----------
   263→    x : NeuroVec
   264→        The 4D data to reduce
   265→    fac : np.ndarray
   266→        Factor array with length equal to number of volumes
   267→    FUN : callable
   268→        Function to apply to each voxel's values within a level
   269→        
   270→    Returns
   271→    -------
   272→    NeuroVol
   273→        Volume containing reduced values
   274→        
   275→    R Equivalent
   276→    ------------
   277→    neuroim2::split_reduce
   278→    """
   279→    # Determine number of volumes (time dimension)
   280→    # For all NeuroVec types, time is last dimension (x, y, z, time)
   281→    n_volumes = x.shape[3]
   282→        
   283→    if len(fac) != n_volumes:
   284→        raise ValueError(f"Length of factor ({len(fac)}) must equal number of volumes ({n_volumes})")
   285→    
   286→    # Create 3D space for output volume
   287→    # For all NeuroVec types, time is last dimension, so spatial dims are first 3
   288→    vol_space = NeuroSpace(dim=x.space.dim[:3], spacing=x.space.spacing[:3], 
   289→                          origin=x.space.origin[:3])
   290→    
   291→    # Initialize output array
   292→    out_data = np.zeros(x.shape[:3])
   293→    
   294→    # For each voxel
   295→    if isinstance(x, DenseNeuroVec):
   296→        # Reshape data to proper 4D shape
   297→        data_4d = x.data.reshape(x.shape)
   298→        
   299→        # Vectorized processing of all voxels
   300→        # Reshape to (n_voxels, n_timepoints)
   301→        n_voxels = np.prod(x.shape[:3])
   302→        n_time = x.shape[3]
   303→        data_2d = data_4d.reshape(n_voxels, n_time, order='F')
   304→        
   305→        # Process all voxels at once
   306→        unique_levels = np.unique(fac)
   307→        for vox_idx in range(n_voxels):
   308→            voxel_series = data_2d[vox_idx, :]
   309→            
   310→            # Group by factor and apply function
   311→            reduced_values = []
   312→            for level in unique_levels:
   313→                level_mask = fac == level
   314→                level_values = voxel_series[level_mask]
   315→                if len(level_values) > 0:
   316→                    reduced_values.append(FUN(level_values))
   317→            
   318→            # Store mean of reduced values
   319→            if reduced_values:
   320→                # Convert voxel index back to 3D coordinates
   321→                coords = np.unravel_index(vox_idx, out_data.shape, order='C')
   322→                out_data[coords] = np.mean(reduced_values)
   323→    else:
   324→        # Handle sparse case
   325→        from .neuro_vec import SparseNeuroVec
   326→        # For sparse, process each stored voxel
   327→        out_indices = []
   328→        out_values = []
   329→        
   330→        # SparseNeuroVec data is (time, voxels)
   331→        for vox_idx in range(x.data.shape[1]):
   332→            voxel_series = x.data[:, vox_idx]
   333→            
   334→            # Group by factor and apply function
   335→            reduced_values = []
   336→            for level in np.unique(fac):
   337→                level_mask = fac == level
   338→                level_values = voxel_series[level_mask]
   339→                if len(level_values) > 0:
   340→                    reduced_values.append(FUN(level_values))
   341→            
   342→            # Store mean of reduced values
   343→            if reduced_values:
   344→                out_indices.append(x._lookup[vox_idx])
   345→                out_values.append(np.mean(reduced_values))
   346→        
   347→        if out_indices:
   348→            return SparseNeuroVol(out_values, vol_space, out_indices)
   349→        else:
   350→            return DenseNeuroVol(np.zeros(vol_space.dim), vol_space)
   351→    
   352→    return DenseNeuroVol(out_data, vol_space)
   353→
   354→
   355→def split_scale(x: NeuroVec, fac: np.ndarray, 
   356→                center: bool = True, scale: bool = True) -> NeuroVec:
   357→    """Scale a NeuroVec within factor levels.
   358→    
   359→    Centers and/or scales the data within each level of a factor,
   360→    returning a new NeuroVec with the transformed data.
   361→    
   362→    Parameters
   363→    ----------
   364→    x : NeuroVec
   365→        The 4D data to scale
   366→    fac : np.ndarray
   367→        Factor array with length equal to number of volumes
   368→    center : bool
   369→        Whether to center (subtract mean) within each level
   370→    scale : bool
   371→        Whether to scale (divide by SD) within each level
   372→        
   373→    Returns
   374→    -------
   375→    NeuroVec
   376→        Scaled NeuroVec
   377→        
   378→    R Equivalent
   379→    ------------
   380→    neuroim2::split_scale
   381→    """
   382→    # Determine number of volumes (time dimension)
   383→    # For all NeuroVec types, time is last dimension (x, y, z, time)
   384→    n_volumes = x.shape[3]
   385→        
   386→    if len(fac) != n_volumes:
   387→        raise ValueError(f"Length of factor ({len(fac)}) must equal number of volumes ({n_volumes})")
   388→    
   389→    if isinstance(x, DenseNeuroVec):
   390→        # Copy data and reshape to 2D (n_voxels, n_timepoints)
   391→        scaled_data = x.data.copy()
   392→        n_voxels = np.prod(x.shape[:3])
   393→        data_2d = scaled_data.reshape(n_voxels, x.shape[3], order='F')
   394→        
   395→        # Process each level
   396→        for level in np.unique(fac):
   397→            level_mask = fac == level
   398→            level_indices = np.where(level_mask)[0]
   399→            
   400→            # Get data for this level (select time points)
   401→            level_data = data_2d[:, level_indices]
   402→            
   403→            if center:
   404→                # Center by subtracting mean across time points
   405→                level_mean = np.mean(level_data, axis=1, keepdims=True)
   406→                level_data = level_data - level_mean
   407→            
   408→            if scale:
   409→                # Scale by dividing by SD across time points
   410→                level_std = np.std(level_data, axis=1, keepdims=True)
   411→                # Avoid division by zero
   412→                level_std[level_std == 0] = 1.0
   413→                level_data = level_data / level_std
   414→            
   415→            # Put back
   416→            data_2d[:, level_indices] = level_data
   417→        
   418→        # Reshape back to 4D
   419→        scaled_4d = data_2d.reshape(x.shape, order='F')
   420→        return DenseNeuroVec(scaled_4d, x.space)
   421→    else:
   422→        # Handle sparse case
   423→        raise NotImplementedError("split_scale not yet implemented for sparse NeuroVec")
   424→
   425→
   426→def partition(x: NeuroVol, k: int, 
   427→              method: str = "kmeans", mask: Optional[LogicalNeuroVol] = None) -> ClusteredNeuroVol:
   428→    """Partition a volume into k regions.
   429→    
   430→    Uses clustering algorithms to partition the brain volume into
   431→    k distinct regions based on voxel values.
   432→    
   433→    Parameters
   434→    ----------
   435→    x : NeuroVol
   436→        The volume to partition
   437→    k : int
   438→        Number of partitions/clusters
   439→    method : str
   440→        Clustering method ("kmeans" or others)
   441→    mask : LogicalNeuroVol, optional
   442→        Mask to restrict clustering to specific voxels
   443→        
   444→    Returns
   445→    -------
   446→    ClusteredNeuroVol
   447→        Clustered volume with partition labels
   448→        
   449→    R Equivalent
   450→    ------------
   451→    neuroim2::partition
   452→    """
   453→    from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
   454→    
   455→    # Get mask - either provided or non-zero voxels
   456→    if mask is not None:
   457→        mask_data = mask.data
   458→    else:
   459→        mask_data = x.data != 0
   460→    
   461→    mask_indices = np.where(mask_data.ravel())[0]
   462→    values = x.data.ravel()[mask_indices].reshape(-1, 1)
   463→    
   464→    if len(values) < k:
   465→        raise ValueError(f"Number of non-zero voxels ({len(values)}) is less than k ({k})")
   466→    
   467→    if method == "kmeans":
   468→        # Perform k-means clustering
   469→        kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=0)
   470→        labels = kmeans.fit_predict(values)
   471→        
   472→        # Create label array
   473→        label_array = np.zeros(x.shape, dtype=int)
   474→        label_array.ravel()[mask_indices] = labels + 1  # 1-indexed
   475→        
   476→        # Create mask volume
   477→        from .neuro_vol import LogicalNeuroVol
   478→        mask_vol = LogicalNeuroVol(mask_data, x.space)
   479→        
   480→        # Create clustered volume
   481→        clustered = ClusteredNeuroVol(
   482→            mask=mask_vol,
   483→            clusters=labels + 1  # 1-indexed like R
   484→        )
   485→        
   486→        return clustered
   487→    else:
   488→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown method: {method}")
   489→
   490→
   491→def map_values(x: NeuroVol, lookup: Dict[float, float]) -> NeuroVol:
   492→    """Map values in a volume using a lookup table.
   493→    
   494→    Replaces values in the volume according to a lookup dictionary.
   495→    
   496→    Parameters
   497→    ----------
   498→    x : NeuroVol
   499→        The volume to transform
   500→    lookup : dict
   501→        Dictionary mapping old values to new values
   502→        
   503→    Returns
   504→    -------
   505→    NeuroVol
   506→        Volume with mapped values
   507→        
   508→    R Equivalent
   509→    ------------
   510→    neuroim2::map_values
   511→    """
   512→    # Create copy of data
   513→    mapped_data = x.data.copy()
   514→    
   515→    # Apply mapping
   516→    for old_val, new_val in lookup.items():
   517→        mapped_data[x.data == old_val] = new_val
   518→    
   519→    # Return same type of volume
   520→    if isinstance(x, SparseNeuroVol):
   521→        # For sparse, only map the non-zero values
   522→        mapped_values = x.data.copy()
   523→        for i, val in enumerate(x.data):
   524→            if val in lookup:
   525→                mapped_values[i] = lookup[val]
   526→        return SparseNeuroVol(
   527→            data=mapped_values,
   528→            space=x.space,
   529→            indices=x.indices
   530→        )
   531→    else:
   532→        # Use type() to preserve the exact class, including subclasses
   533→        return type(x)(mapped_data, x.space)
   534→
   535→
   536→def centroids(x: ClusteredNeuroVol, method: str = "center_of_mass") -> Dict[int, np.ndarray]:
   537→    """Calculate centroids for each cluster.
   538→    
   539→    Computes the centroid (center point) of each cluster using
   540→    the specified method.
   541→    
   542→    Parameters
   543→    ----------
   544→    x : ClusteredNeuroVol
   545→        Clustered volume
   546→    method : str
   547→        Method for computing centroids ("center_of_mass" or "median")
   548→        
   549→    Returns
   550→    -------
   551→    dict
   552→        Dictionary mapping cluster IDs to centroid coordinates
   553→        
   554→    R Equivalent
   555→    ------------
   556→    neuroim2::centroids
   557→    """
   558→    if method == "center_of_mass":
   559→        # Use existing method
   560→        return x.cluster_centers()
   561→    elif method == "median":
   562→        # Compute median coordinates
   563→        centers = {}
   564→        # Get the full volume indices where mask is True
   565→        mask_indices = np.where(x.mask.data.ravel())[0]
   566→        
   567→        for cluster_id, cluster_data_indices in x.cluster_map.items():
   568→            # cluster_data_indices are indices into the masked data
   569→            # We need to convert them to full volume indices
   570→            full_volume_indices = mask_indices[cluster_data_indices]
   571→            # Convert to 3D coordinates
   572→            coords = np.array(np.unravel_index(full_volume_indices, x.mask.shape)).T
   573→            # Median of each dimension
   574→            center = np.median(coords, axis=0)
   575→            centers[cluster_id] = center
   576→        return centers
   577→    else:
   578→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown method: {method}")
