<cell id="cell-0"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type># Working with Image Volumes

This tutorial covers the basics of working with 3D neuroimaging volumes in pyneuroim.

## Reading a NIFTI formatted image volume

The primary way to read a volumetric image file is to use `read_vol()`</cell id="cell-0">
<cell id="cell-1"># Import required libraries
import pyneuroim as pn
import numpy as np

# Create example data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create example volume
space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), 
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))
example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)
example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())

# Create volume
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)</cell id="cell-1">
<cell id="cell-2"># Note: In practice, you would read a NIFTI file like this:
# vol = pn.read_vol("path/to/image.nii.gz")</cell id="cell-2">
<cell id="cell-3"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Working with image volumes</cell id="cell-3">
<cell id="cell-4">print(vol)
# DenseNeuroVol
#   Type      : DenseNeuroVol
#   Dimension : 64 X 64 X 25
#   Spacing   : 3.5 X 3.5 X 5.0
#   Origin    : -110.5, -88.9342, -42.75
#   Range     : [0.000, 1.000]</cell id="cell-4">
<cell id="cell-5"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>`read_vol()` returns a `NeuroVol` object which extends a numpy array and has 3 dimensions (x, y, z)</cell id="cell-5">
<cell id="cell-6">print(type(vol))
print(isinstance(vol.data, np.ndarray))
print(vol.shape)
# Access individual voxels
print(vol[0, 0, 0])
# 0.0
print(vol[63, 63, 24])
# 0.0</cell id="cell-6">
<cell id="cell-7"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Arithmetic operations</cell id="cell-7">
<cell id="cell-8"># Add two volumes
vol2 = vol + vol
assert np.sum(vol2.data) == 2 * np.sum(vol.data)
# Subtract and multiply
vol3 = vol2 - 2 * vol
assert np.all(vol3.data == 0)
# Element-wise operations
vol4 = vol * 2.5
vol5 = vol + 10
vol6 = vol ** 2</cell id="cell-8">
<cell id="cell-9"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Converting to logical (binary) volumes</cell id="cell-9">
<cell id="cell-10"># Convert to logical volume
vol_binary = vol.as_logical()
print(type(vol_binary))
print(vol_binary[0, 0, 0])</cell id="cell-10">
<cell id="cell-11"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Creating volumes from arrays</cell id="cell-11">
<cell id="cell-12"># Create a volume from a numpy array
x = np.zeros((64, 64, 64))</cell id="cell-12">
<cell id="cell-13"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Now we create a `NeuroSpace` instance that describes the geometry of the image including its dimensions and voxel spacing</cell id="cell-13">
<cell id="cell-14"># Create a NeuroSpace instance that describes the geometry of the image
bspace = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=[64, 64, 64], spacing=[1, 1, 1])
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(x, bspace)
print(vol)</cell id="cell-14">
<cell id="cell-15"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Working with existing spaces</cell id="cell-15">
<cell id="cell-16"># Create a new volume with the same space as an existing one
vol2 = pn.DenseNeuroVol((vol.data + 1) * 25, vol.space)
print(f"Max value: {np.max(vol2.data)}")
print(f"Volume space: {vol2.space}")</cell id="cell-16">
<cell id="cell-17"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Writing a NIFTI formatted image volume</cell id="cell-17">
<cell id="cell-18"># Write NIFTI formatted image volume
# Note: In practice, you would specify actual file paths
# pn.write_vol(vol2, "output.nii")
# pn.write_vol(vol2, "output.nii.gz")
print("In practice, use pn.write_vol() to save volumes")</cell id="cell-18">
<cell id="cell-19"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Sparse volumes</cell id="cell-19">
<cell id="cell-20"># Create a sparse volume with only a few non-zero values
data = np.zeros((64, 64, 64))
data[10:20, 10:20, 10:20] = 1.0

# Convert to sparse
dense_vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(data, bspace)
sparse_vol = dense_vol.as_sparse()
print(f"Volume type: {type(sparse_vol)}")

# Sparse volumes behave like dense volumes
print(f"Value at [15, 15, 15]: {sparse_vol[15, 15, 15]}")
print(f"Value at [0, 0, 0]: {sparse_vol[0, 0, 0]}")</cell id="cell-20">
<cell id="cell-21"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Volume statistics</cell id="cell-21">
<cell id="cell-22"># Create a volume with random data
data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 64)
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(data, bspace)

# Get statistics
print(f"Mean: {vol.mean():.3f}")
print(f"SD: {vol.std():.3f}")
print(f"Min: {vol.min():.3f}")
print(f"Max: {vol.max():.3f}")

# Find location of minimum and maximum
min_idx = vol.which_min()
max_idx = vol.which_max()

# Convert indices to coordinates
min_coords = vol.space.index_to_grid(min_idx)
max_coords = vol.space.index_to_grid(max_idx)
print(f"Min at grid coordinates: {min_coords[0]}")
print(f"Max at grid coordinates: {max_coords[0]}")</cell id="cell-22">

# Working with Image Volumes

This tutorial covers the basics of working with 3D neuroimaging volumes in pyneuroim.

## Reading a NIFTI formatted image volume

The primary way to read a volumetric image file is to use `read_vol()`

# Import required libraries
import pyneuroim as pn
import numpy as np

# Create example data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create example volume
space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), 
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))
example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)
example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())

# Create volume
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)

# Note: In practice, you would read a NIFTI file like this:
# vol = pn.read_vol("path/to/image.nii.gz")
Working with image volumes

print(vol)
# DenseNeuroVol
#   Type      : DenseNeuroVol
#   Dimension : 64 X 64 X 25
#   Spacing   : 3.5 X 3.5 X 5.0
#   Origin    : -110.5, -88.9342, -42.75
#   Range     : [0.000, 1.000]
`read_vol()` returns a `NeuroVol` object which extends a numpy array and has 3 dimensions (x, y, z)

print(type(vol))
print(isinstance(vol.data, np.ndarray))
print(vol.shape)
# Access individual voxels
print(vol[0, 0, 0])
# 0.0
print(vol[63, 63, 24])
# 0.0
Arithmetic operations

# Add two volumes
vol2 = vol + vol
assert np.sum(vol2.data) == 2 * np.sum(vol.data)
# Subtract and multiply
vol3 = vol2 - 2 * vol
assert np.all(vol3.data == 0)
# Element-wise operations
vol4 = vol * 2.5
vol5 = vol + 10
vol6 = vol ** 2
Converting to logical (binary) volumes

# Convert to logical volume
vol_binary = vol.as_logical()
print(type(vol_binary))
print(vol_binary[0, 0, 0])
Creating volumes from arrays

# Create a volume from a numpy array
x = np.zeros((64, 64, 64))
Now we create a `NeuroSpace` instance that describes the geometry of the image including its dimensions and voxel spacing

# Create a NeuroSpace instance that describes the geometry of the image
bspace = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=[64, 64, 64], spacing=[1, 1, 1])
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(x, bspace)
print(vol)
Working with existing spaces

# Create a new volume with the same space as an existing one
vol2 = pn.DenseNeuroVol((vol.data + 1) * 25, vol.space)
print(f"Max value: {np.max(vol2.data)}")
print(f"Volume space: {vol2.space}")
Writing a NIFTI formatted image volume

# Write NIFTI formatted image volume
# Note: In practice, you would specify actual file paths
# pn.write_vol(vol2, "output.nii")
# pn.write_vol(vol2, "output.nii.gz")
print("In practice, use pn.write_vol() to save volumes")
Sparse volumes

# Create a sparse volume with only a few non-zero values
data = np.zeros((64, 64, 64))
data[10:20, 10:20, 10:20] = 1.0

# Convert to sparse
dense_vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(data, bspace)
sparse_vol = dense_vol.as_sparse()
print(f"Volume type: {type(sparse_vol)}")

# Sparse volumes behave like dense volumes
print(f"Value at [15, 15, 15]: {sparse_vol[15, 15, 15]}")
print(f"Value at [0, 0, 0]: {sparse_vol[0, 0, 0]}")
Volume statistics

# Create a volume with random data
data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 64)
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(data, bspace)

# Get statistics
print(f"Mean: {vol.mean():.3f}")
print(f"SD: {vol.std():.3f}")
print(f"Min: {vol.min():.3f}")
print(f"Max: {vol.max():.3f}")

# Find location of minimum and maximum
min_idx = vol.which_min()
max_idx = vol.which_max()

# Convert indices to coordinates
min_coords = vol.space.index_to_grid(min_idx)
max_coords = vol.space.index_to_grid(max_idx)
print(f"Min at grid coordinates: {min_coords[0]}")
print(f"Max at grid coordinates: {max_coords[0]}")

<cell id="cell-0"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type># Four-dimensional Neuroimaging Data

The pyneuroim package contains data structures and functions for reading, accessing, and processing 4-dimensional neuroimaging data.

## Reading a four-dimensional NIFTI image

To read a 4D image, use the `read_vec()` function</cell id="cell-0">
<cell id="cell-1">import pyneuroim
import numpy as np
# Read a single 4D NIFTI file
vec = pyneuroim.read_vec("path/to/4d_image.nii")
print(vec.shape)
print(vec)
# DenseNeuroVec
#   Type      : DenseNeuroVec
#   Dimension : 64 X 64 X 25 X 100
#   Spacing   : 3.5 X 3.5 X 5.0 X 2.0
#   Origin    : -110.5, -88.9342, -42.75, 0.0</cell id="cell-1">
<cell id="cell-2"># Create example data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create example volume
space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), 
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))
example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)
example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())

# Create example 4D data
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100))
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# For tutorials, we'll create objects directly instead of reading files
# vol = pn.read_vol("path/to/image.nii.gz")  # In practice
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)  # For tutorial</cell id="cell-2">
<cell id="cell-3"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Reading multiple 4D images</cell id="cell-3">
<cell id="cell-4">file_list = ["image1.nii", "image2.nii", "image3.nii"]
vec = pyneuroim.read_vec(file_list)
print(vec.shape)
# Will concatenate along the time dimension</cell id="cell-4">
<cell id="cell-5"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Extracting subsets of volumes</cell id="cell-5">
<cell id="cell-6">vec_subset = vec.sub_vector(slice(0, 6))
print(vec_subset.shape)
# Extract specific volumes
vec_subset = vec.sub_vector([0, 2, 4, 6])
print(vec_subset.shape)</cell id="cell-6">
<cell id="cell-7"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Extracting time series data</cell id="cell-7">
<cell id="cell-8">ts = vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(ts.shape)
# Plot the time series
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot(ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Signal')
plt.title('Time series at voxel (10, 10, 10)')</cell id="cell-8">
<cell id="cell-9"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Multiple voxel time series</cell id="cell-9">
<cell id="cell-10">coords = np.array([[10, 10, 10],
[20, 20, 10],
[30, 30, 10]])
ts_matrix = vec.series(coords)
print(ts_matrix.shape)</cell id="cell-10">
<cell id="cell-11"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>ROI-based time series extraction</cell id="cell-11">
<cell id="cell-12">center = [32, 32, 12]
roi = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vec, center, radius=8)
# Extract time series for all voxels in the ROI
roi_series = vec.series_roi(roi)
print(roi_series.shape)</cell id="cell-12">
<cell id="cell-13"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Using linear indices</cell id="cell-13">
<cell id="cell-14">roi_series = vec.series(np.arange(100))
print(roi_series.shape)</cell id="cell-14">
<cell id="cell-15"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Working with masks</cell id="cell-15">
<cell id="cell-16"># Load a binary mask
mask = pyneuroim.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")
# Get indices of non-zero voxels
mask_indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel(order='F'))[0]
# Extract time series for masked voxels
masked_series = vec.series(mask_indices)
print(f"Extracted {masked_series.shape[1]} voxels from mask")</cell id="cell-16">
<cell id="cell-17"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Converting between formats</cell id="cell-17">
<cell id="cell-18"># Convert to sparse representation using a mask
mask_vol = pyneuroim.LogicalNeuroVol(mask.data > 0, mask.space)
sparse_vec = vec.as_sparse(mask_vol)
print(type(sparse_vec))
# Sparse vectors work the same way
ts = sparse_vec.series(10, 10, 10)</cell id="cell-18">
<cell id="cell-19"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Converting to matrix format</cell id="cell-19">
<cell id="cell-20"># Get as voxels x time matrix
matrix = vec.as_matrix()
print(matrix.shape)</cell id="cell-20">
<cell id="cell-21"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Concatenating NeuroVecs</cell id="cell-21">
<cell id="cell-22"># Create some example vectors
vec1 = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50))
vec2 = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 30))
# Concatenate
combined = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(combined.shape)</cell id="cell-22">
<cell id="cell-23"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Extracting individual volumes</cell id="cell-23">
<cell id="cell-24"># Get the first volume
vol0 = vec[..., 0]
print(type(vol0))
# Get multiple volumes as a list
vols = vec.vols([0, 10, 20, 30])
print(len(vols))
# 4</cell id="cell-24">
<cell id="cell-25"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Time series preprocessing</cell id="cell-25">
<cell id="cell-26"># Center and scale each time series
vec_scaled = vec.scale_series(center=True, scale=True)
# Verify: each voxel should have mean≈0, std≈1
ts = vec_scaled.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Mean: {np.mean(ts):.6f}, Std: {np.std(ts):.6f}")</cell id="cell-26">
<cell id="cell-27"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Memory-mapped vectors</cell id="cell-27">
<cell id="cell-28"># Create a memory-mapped vector
big_data = np.random.randn(100, 100, 50, 1000)  # Large dataset
space = pyneuroim.NeuroSpace(dim=[100, 100, 50, 1000])
# This keeps data on disk
big_vec = pyneuroim.BigNeuroVec(big_data, space)
# Access works the same way
ts = big_vec.series(50, 50, 25)
print(ts.shape)
# Data stays on disk until accessed
print(big_vec)
#   ...</cell id="cell-28">

print(ts_matrix.shape)
ROI-based time series extraction

center = [32, 32, 12]
roi = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vec, center, radius=8)
# Extract time series for all voxels in the ROI
roi_series = vec.series_roi(roi)
print(roi_series.shape)
Using linear indices

roi_series = vec.series(np.arange(100))
print(roi_series.shape)
Working with masks

# Load a binary mask
mask = pyneuroim.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")
# Get indices of non-zero voxels
mask_indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel(order='F'))[0]
# Extract time series for masked voxels
masked_series = vec.series(mask_indices)
print(f"Extracted {masked_series.shape[1]} voxels from mask")
Converting between formats

# Convert to sparse representation using a mask
mask_vol = pyneuroim.LogicalNeuroVol(mask.data > 0, mask.space)
sparse_vec = vec.as_sparse(mask_vol)
print(type(sparse_vec))
# Sparse vectors work the same way
ts = sparse_vec.series(10, 10, 10)
Converting to matrix format

# Get as voxels x time matrix
matrix = vec.as_matrix()
print(matrix.shape)
Concatenating NeuroVecs

# Create some example vectors
vec1 = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50))
vec2 = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 30))
# Concatenate
combined = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(combined.shape)
Extracting individual volumes

# Get the first volume
vol0 = vec[..., 0]
print(type(vol0))
# Get multiple volumes as a list
vols = vec.vols([0, 10, 20, 30])
print(len(vols))
# 4
Time series preprocessing

# Center and scale each time series
vec_scaled = vec.scale_series(center=True, scale=True)
# Verify: each voxel should have mean≈0, std≈1
ts = vec_scaled.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Mean: {np.mean(ts):.6f}, Std: {np.std(ts):.6f}")
Memory-mapped vectors

# Create a memory-mapped vector
big_data = np.random.randn(100, 100, 50, 1000)  # Large dataset
space = pyneuroim.NeuroSpace(dim=[100, 100, 50, 1000])
# This keeps data on disk
big_vec = pyneuroim.BigNeuroVec(big_data, space)
# Access works the same way
ts = big_vec.series(50, 50, 25)
print(ts.shape)
# Data stays on disk until accessed
print(big_vec)
#   ...

<cell id="cell-0"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type># Four-dimensional Neuroimaging Data

The pyneuroim package contains data structures and functions for reading, accessing, and processing 4-dimensional neuroimaging data.

## Reading a four-dimensional NIFTI image

To read a 4D image, use the `read_vec()` function</cell id="cell-0">
<cell id="cell-1"># Import required libraries
import pyneuroim as pn
import numpy as np

# Create example 4D data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create a 4D space (64x64x25 spatial, 100 time points)
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100), 
                         spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0, 2.0),
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75, 0.0))

# Create example 4D data with some temporal structure
np.random.seed(42)
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# Create a 4D NeuroVec
vec = pn.DenseNeuroVec(example_4d_data, space_4d)

# Note: In practice, you would read a 4D NIFTI file like this:
# vec = pn.read_vec("path/to/4d_image.nii")

print(vec.shape)
print(vec)</cell id="cell-1">
<cell id="cell-2"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Reading multiple 4D images</cell id="cell-2">
<cell id="cell-3">file_list = ["image1.nii", "image2.nii", "image3.nii"]
vec = pyneuroim.read_vec(file_list)
print(vec.shape)
# Will concatenate along the time dimension</cell id="cell-3">
<cell id="cell-4"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type># Create multiple example 4D images
file_list = ["image1.nii", "image2.nii", "image3.nii"]

# In practice, you would read multiple files:
# vec = pn.read_vec(file_list)

# For this tutorial, let's create and concatenate multiple 4D datasets
vec1 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))
vec2 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))

# Concatenate along time dimension
vec_concat = vec1.concat(vec2)

toolUseResult.source:
# Four-dimensional Neuroimaging Data

The pyneuroim package contains data structures and functions for reading, accessing, and processing 4-dimensional neuroimaging data.

## Reading a four-dimensional NIFTI image

To read a 4D image, use the `read_vec()` function

# Import required libraries
import pyneuroim as pn
import numpy as np

# Create example 4D data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create a 4D space (64x64x25 spatial, 100 time points)
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100), 
                         spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0, 2.0),
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75, 0.0))

# Create example 4D data with some temporal structure
np.random.seed(42)
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# Create a 4D NeuroVec
vec = pn.DenseNeuroVec(example_4d_data, space_4d)

# Note: In practice, you would read a 4D NIFTI file like this:
# vec = pn.read_vec("path/to/4d_image.nii")

print(vec.shape)
print(vec)
Reading multiple 4D images

file_list = ["image1.nii", "image2.nii", "image3.nii"]
vec = pyneuroim.read_vec(file_list)
print(vec.shape)
# Will concatenate along the time dimension

# Create multiple example 4D images
file_list = ["image1.nii", "image2.nii", "image3.nii"]

# In practice, you would read multiple files:
# vec = pn.read_vec(file_list)

# For this tutorial, let's create and concatenate multiple 4D datasets
vec1 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))
vec2 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))

# Concatenate along time dimension
vec_concat = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(f"Concatenated shape: {vec_concat.shape}")
# Will concatenate along the time dimension

vec_subset = vec.sub_vector(slice(0, 6))
print(vec_subset.shape)
# Extract specific volumes
vec_subset = vec.sub_vector([0, 2, 4, 6])
print(vec_subset.shape)

# Extract subset of volumes
vec_subset = vec.sub_vector(slice(0, 6))
print(f"Subset shape (first 6 volumes): {vec_subset.shape}")

# Extract specific volumes by indices
vec_subset = vec.sub_vector([0, 2, 4, 6])
print(f"Subset shape (specific volumes): {vec_subset.shape}")

ts = vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(ts.shape)
# Plot the time series
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot(ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Signal')
plt.title('Time series at voxel (10, 10, 10)')

# Extract time series from a single voxel
ts = vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Time series shape: {ts.shape}")

# Plot the time series
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
plt.plot(ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Signal')
plt.title('Time series at voxel (10, 10, 10)')
plt.show()

coords = np.array([[10, 10, 10],
[20, 20, 10],
[30, 30, 10]])
ts_matrix = vec.series(coords)
print(ts_matrix.shape)

# Extract time series from multiple voxels
coords = np.array([[10, 10, 10],
                   [20, 20, 10],
                   [30, 30, 10]])
                   
ts_matrix = vec.series(coords)
print(f"Time series matrix shape: {ts_matrix.shape}")
print(f"(time points x voxels): ({ts_matrix.shape[0]} x {ts_matrix.shape[1]})")

center = [32, 32, 12]
roi = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vec, center, radius=8)
# Extract time series for all voxels in the ROI
roi_series = vec.series_roi(roi)
print(roi_series.shape)

# Create a spherical ROI
center = [32, 32, 12]
roi = pn.spherical_roi(vec.space, center, radius=8)

# Extract time series for all voxels in the ROI
roi_series = vec.series_roi(roi)
print(f"ROI time series shape: {roi_series.shape}")
print(f"Number of voxels in ROI: {roi_series.shape[1]}")

roi_series = vec.series(np.arange(100))
print(roi_series.shape)

# Extract time series using linear indices
# First 100 voxels
roi_series = vec.series(np.arange(100))
print(f"Time series shape for first 100 voxels: {roi_series.shape}")

# Load a binary mask
mask = pyneuroim.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")
# Get indices of non-zero voxels
mask_indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel(order='F'))[0]
# Extract time series for masked voxels
masked_series = vec.series(mask_indices)
print(f"Extracted {masked_series.shape[1]} voxels from mask")

# Create a binary mask for tutorial
# In practice: mask = pn.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")

# Create example mask (sphere in center of brain)
mask_data = np.zeros((64, 64, 25))
center = np.array([32, 32, 12])
for i in range(64):
    for j in range(64):
        for k in range(25):
            if np.sqrt((i-center[0])**2 + (j-center[1])**2 + (k-center[2])**2) <= 15:
                mask_data[i, j, k] = 1

mask = pn.LogicalNeuroVol(mask_data, vec.space.sub_space())

# Get indices of non-zero voxels
mask_indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel(order='F'))[0]

# Extract time series for masked voxels
masked_series = vec.series(mask_indices)
print(f"Extracted {masked_series.shape[1]} voxels from mask")

# Convert to sparse representation using a mask
mask_vol = pyneuroim.LogicalNeuroVol(mask.data > 0, mask.space)
sparse_vec = vec.as_sparse(mask_vol)
print(type(sparse_vec))
# Sparse vectors work the same way
ts = sparse_vec.series(10, 10, 10)

# Convert to sparse representation using a mask
mask_vol = pn.LogicalNeuroVol(mask.data > 0, mask.space)
sparse_vec = vec.as_sparse(mask_vol)
print(f"Sparse vector type: {type(sparse_vec)}")

# Sparse vectors work the same way
ts = sparse_vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Time series from sparse vector: shape={ts.shape}")

# Get as voxels x time matrix
matrix = vec.as_matrix()
print(matrix.shape)

# Get as voxels x time matrix
matrix = vec.as_matrix()
print(f"Matrix shape: {matrix.shape}")
print(f"(voxels x time): ({matrix.shape[0]} x {matrix.shape[1]})")

# Create some example vectors
vec1 = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50))
vec2 = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 30))
# Concatenate
combined = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(combined.shape)

# Create some example vectors with matching spatial dimensions
space1 = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(10, 10, 10, 50))
space2 = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(10, 10, 10, 30))

vec1 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50), space1)
vec2 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 30), space2)

# Concatenate
combined = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(f"Combined shape: {combined.shape}")
print(f"Original shapes: {vec1.shape} + {vec2.shape} = {combined.shape}")

# Get the first volume
vol0 = vec[..., 0]
print(type(vol0))
# Get multiple volumes as a list
vols = vec.vols([0, 10, 20, 30])
print(len(vols))
# 4

# Get the first volume
vol0 = vec[..., 0]
print(f"First volume type: {type(vol0)}")
print(f"First volume shape: {vol0.shape}")

# Get multiple volumes as a list
vols = vec.vols([0, 10, 20, 30])
print(f"Number of volumes extracted: {len(vols)}")
print(f"Each volume shape: {vols[0].shape}")

# Center and scale each time series
vec_scaled = vec.scale_series(center=True, scale=True)
# Verify: each voxel should have mean≈0, std≈1
ts = vec_scaled.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Mean: {np.mean(ts):.6f}, Std: {np.std(ts):.6f}")

# Center and scale each time series
vec_scaled = vec.scale_series(center=True, scale=True)

# Verify: each voxel should have mean≈0, std≈1
ts = vec_scaled.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Mean: {np.mean(ts):.6f}, Std: {np.std(ts):.6f}")

# Check a few more voxels
for coord in [(20, 20, 10), (30, 30, 15)]:
    ts = vec_scaled.series(*coord)
    print(f"Voxel {coord}: Mean={np.mean(ts):.6f}, Std={np.std(ts):.6f}")

# Create a memory-mapped vector
big_data = np.random.randn(100, 100, 50, 1000)  # Large dataset
space = pyneuroim.NeuroSpace(dim=[100, 100, 50, 1000])
# This keeps data on disk
big_vec = pyneuroim.BigNeuroVec(big_data, space)
# Access works the same way
ts = big_vec.series(50, 50, 25)
print(ts.shape)
# Data stays on disk until accessed
print(big_vec)
#   ...

# Four-dimensional Neuroimaging Data

The pyneuroim package contains data structures and functions for reading, accessing, and processing 4-dimensional neuroimaging data.

## Reading a four-dimensional NIFTI image

To read a 4D image, use the `read_vec()` function

# Import required libraries
import pyneuroim as pn
import numpy as np

# Create example 4D data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create a 4D space (64x64x25 spatial, 100 time points)
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100), 
                         spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0, 2.0),
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75, 0.0))

# Create example 4D data with some temporal structure
np.random.seed(42)
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# Create a 4D NeuroVec
vec = pn.DenseNeuroVec(example_4d_data, space_4d)

# Note: In practice, you would read a 4D NIFTI file like this:
# vec = pn.read_vec("path/to/4d_image.nii")

print(vec.shape)
print(vec)
Reading multiple 4D images

# Create multiple example 4D images
file_list = ["image1.nii", "image2.nii", "image3.nii"]

# In practice, you would read multiple files:
# vec = pn.read_vec(file_list)

# For this tutorial, let's create and concatenate multiple 4D datasets
vec1 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))
vec2 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))

# Concatenate along time dimension
vec_concat = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(f"Concatenated shape: {vec_concat.shape}")
# Will concatenate along the time dimension

# Create multiple example 4D images
file_list = ["image1.nii", "image2.nii", "image3.nii"]

# In practice, you would read multiple files:
# vec = pn.read_vec(file_list)

# For this tutorial, let's create and concatenate multiple 4D datasets
vec1 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))
vec2 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))

# Concatenate along time dimension
vec_concat = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(f"Concatenated shape: {vec_concat.shape}")
# Will concatenate along the time dimension

ts = vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(ts.shape)
# Plot the time series
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot(ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Signal')
plt.title('Time series at voxel (10, 10, 10)')

# Extract subset of volumes
vec_subset = vec.sub_vector(slice(0, 6))
print(f"Subset shape (first 6 volumes): {vec_subset.shape}")

# Extract specific volumes by indices
vec_subset = vec.sub_vector([0, 2, 4, 6])
print(f"Subset shape (specific volumes): {vec_subset.shape}")

# Extract time series from multiple voxels
coords = np.array([[10, 10, 10],
                   [20, 20, 10],
                   [30, 30, 10]])
                   
ts_matrix = vec.series(coords)
print(f"Time series matrix shape: {ts_matrix.shape}")
print(f"(time points x voxels): ({ts_matrix.shape[0]} x {ts_matrix.shape[1]})")

# Extract time series from a single voxel
ts = vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Time series shape: {ts.shape}")

# Plot the time series
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
plt.plot(ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Signal')
plt.title('Time series at voxel (10, 10, 10)')
plt.show()

roi_series = vec.series(np.arange(100))
print(roi_series.shape)

# Extract time series from multiple voxels
coords = np.array([[10, 10, 10],
                   [20, 20, 10],
                   [30, 30, 10]])
                   
ts_matrix = vec.series(coords)
print(f"Time series matrix shape: {ts_matrix.shape}")
print(f"(time points x voxels): ({ts_matrix.shape[0]} x {ts_matrix.shape[1]})")

# Create a binary mask for tutorial
# In practice: mask = pn.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")

# Create example mask (sphere in center of brain)
mask_data = np.zeros((64, 64, 25))
center = np.array([32, 32, 12])
for i in range(64):
    for j in range(64):
        for k in range(25):
            if np.sqrt((i-center[0])**2 + (j-center[1])**2 + (k-center[2])**2) <= 15:
                mask_data[i, j, k] = 1

mask = pn.LogicalNeuroVol(mask_data, vec.space.sub_space())

# Get indices of non-zero voxels
mask_indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel(order='F'))[0]

# Extract time series for masked voxels
masked_series = vec.series(mask_indices)
print(f"Extracted {masked_series.shape[1]} voxels from mask")

# Create a spherical ROI
center = [32, 32, 12]
roi = pn.spherical_roi(vec.space, center, radius=8)

# Extract time series for all voxels in the ROI
roi_series = vec.series_roi(roi)
print(f"ROI time series shape: {roi_series.shape}")
print(f"Number of voxels in ROI: {roi_series.shape[1]}")

# Get as voxels x time matrix
matrix = vec.as_matrix()
print(matrix.shape)

# Extract time series using linear indices
# First 100 voxels
roi_series = vec.series(np.arange(100))
print(f"Time series shape for first 100 voxels: {roi_series.shape}")

# Create some example vectors with matching spatial dimensions
space1 = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(10, 10, 10, 50))
space2 = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(10, 10, 10, 30))

vec1 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50), space1)
vec2 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 30), space2)

# Concatenate
combined = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(f"Combined shape: {combined.shape}")
print(f"Original shapes: {vec1.shape} + {vec2.shape} = {combined.shape}")

# Create a binary mask for tutorial
# In practice: mask = pn.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")

# Create example mask (sphere in center of brain)
mask_data = np.zeros((64, 64, 25))
center = np.array([32, 32, 12])
for i in range(64):
    for j in range(64):
        for k in range(25):
            if np.sqrt((i-center[0])**2 + (j-center[1])**2 + (k-center[2])**2) <= 15:
                mask_data[i, j, k] = 1

mask = pn.LogicalNeuroVol(mask_data, vec.space.sub_space())

# Get indices of non-zero voxels
mask_indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel(order='F'))[0]

# Extract time series for masked voxels
masked_series = vec.series(mask_indices)
print(f"Extracted {masked_series.shape[1]} voxels from mask")

# Center and scale each time series
vec_scaled = vec.scale_series(center=True, scale=True)
# Verify: each voxel should have mean≈0, std≈1
ts = vec_scaled.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Mean: {np.mean(ts):.6f}, Std: {np.std(ts):.6f}")

# Convert to sparse representation using a mask
mask_vol = pn.LogicalNeuroVol(mask.data > 0, mask.space)
sparse_vec = vec.as_sparse(mask_vol)
print(f"Sparse vector type: {type(sparse_vec)}")

# Sparse vectors work the same way
ts = sparse_vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Time series from sparse vector: shape={ts.shape}")

toolUseResult.source:
# Four-dimensional Neuroimaging Data

The pyneuroim package contains data structures and functions for reading, accessing, and processing 4-dimensional neuroimaging data.

## Reading a four-dimensional NIFTI image

To read a 4D image, use the `read_vec()` function

# Import required libraries
import pyneuroim as pn
import numpy as np

# Create example 4D data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create a 4D space (64x64x25 spatial, 100 time points)
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100), 
                         spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0, 2.0),
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75, 0.0))

# Create example 4D data with some temporal structure
np.random.seed(42)
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# Create a 4D NeuroVec
vec = pn.DenseNeuroVec(example_4d_data, space_4d)

# Note: In practice, you would read a 4D NIFTI file like this:
# vec = pn.read_vec("path/to/4d_image.nii")

print(vec.shape)
print(vec)
Reading multiple 4D images

# Create multiple example 4D images
file_list = ["image1.nii", "image2.nii", "image3.nii"]

# In practice, you would read multiple files:
# vec = pn.read_vec(file_list)

# For this tutorial, let's create and concatenate multiple 4D datasets
vec1 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))
vec2 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 50), 
                        pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 50)))

# Concatenate along time dimension
vec_concat = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(f"Concatenated shape: {vec_concat.shape}")
# Will concatenate along the time dimension

ts = vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(ts.shape)
# Plot the time series
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot(ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Signal')
plt.title('Time series at voxel (10, 10, 10)')

# Extract time series from multiple voxels
coords = np.array([[10, 10, 10],
                   [20, 20, 10],
                   [30, 30, 10]])
                   
ts_matrix = vec.series(coords)
print(f"Time series matrix shape: {ts_matrix.shape}")
print(f"(time points x voxels): ({ts_matrix.shape[0]} x {ts_matrix.shape[1]})")
## Extracting subsets of volumes

# Extract time series from a single voxel
ts = vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Time series shape: {ts.shape}")

# Plot the time series
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
plt.plot(ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Signal')
plt.title('Time series at voxel (10, 10, 10)')
plt.show()

roi_series = vec.series(np.arange(100))
print(roi_series.shape)

# Extract time series from multiple voxels
coords = np.array([[10, 10, 10],
                   [20, 20, 10],
                   [30, 30, 10]])
                   
ts_matrix = vec.series(coords)
print(f"Time series matrix shape: {ts_matrix.shape}")
print(f"(time points x voxels): ({ts_matrix.shape[0]} x {ts_matrix.shape[1]})")

# Create a binary mask for tutorial
# In practice: mask = pn.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")

# Create example mask (sphere in center of brain)
mask_data = np.zeros((64, 64, 25))
center = np.array([32, 32, 12])
for i in range(64):
    for j in range(64):
        for k in range(25):
            if np.sqrt((i-center[0])**2 + (j-center[1])**2 + (k-center[2])**2) <= 15:
                mask_data[i, j, k] = 1

mask = pn.LogicalNeuroVol(mask_data, vec.space.sub_space())

# Get indices of non-zero voxels
mask_indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel(order='F'))[0]

# Extract time series for masked voxels
masked_series = vec.series(mask_indices)
print(f"Extracted {masked_series.shape[1]} voxels from mask")

# Create a spherical ROI
center = [32, 32, 12]
roi = pn.spherical_roi(vec.space, center, radius=8)

# Extract time series for all voxels in the ROI
roi_series = vec.series_roi(roi)
print(f"ROI time series shape: {roi_series.shape}")
print(f"Number of voxels in ROI: {roi_series.shape[1]}")

# Get as voxels x time matrix
matrix = vec.as_matrix()
print(matrix.shape)

# Extract time series using linear indices
# First 100 voxels
roi_series = vec.series(np.arange(100))
print(f"Time series shape for first 100 voxels: {roi_series.shape}")

# Create some example vectors with matching spatial dimensions
space1 = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(10, 10, 10, 50))
space2 = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(10, 10, 10, 30))

vec1 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50), space1)
vec2 = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 30), space2)

# Concatenate
combined = vec1.concat(vec2)
print(f"Combined shape: {combined.shape}")
print(f"Original shapes: {vec1.shape} + {vec2.shape} = {combined.shape}")

# Create a binary mask for tutorial
# In practice: mask = pn.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")

# Create example mask (sphere in center of brain)
mask_data = np.zeros((64, 64, 25))
center = np.array([32, 32, 12])
for i in range(64):
    for j in range(64):
        for k in range(25):
            if np.sqrt((i-center[0])**2 + (j-center[1])**2 + (k-center[2])**2) <= 15:
                mask_data[i, j, k] = 1

mask = pn.LogicalNeuroVol(mask_data, vec.space.sub_space())

# Get indices of non-zero voxels
mask_indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel(order='F'))[0]

# Extract time series for masked voxels
masked_series = vec.series(mask_indices)
print(f"Extracted {masked_series.shape[1]} voxels from mask")

# Center and scale each time series
vec_scaled = vec.scale_series(center=True, scale=True)
# Verify: each voxel should have mean≈0, std≈1
ts = vec_scaled.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Mean: {np.mean(ts):.6f}, Std: {np.std(ts):.6f}")
## Converting between formats

# Convert to sparse representation using a mask
mask_vol = pn.LogicalNeuroVol(mask.data > 0, mask.space)
sparse_vec = vec.as_sparse(mask_vol)
print(f"Sparse vector type: {type(sparse_vec)}")

# Sparse vectors work the same way
ts = sparse_vec.series(10, 10, 10)
print(f"Time series from sparse vector: shape={ts.shape}")

content.text: Now let me fix the regions_of_interest.ipynb notebook:

<cell id="cell-0"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type># Regions of Interest

In pyneuroim there is comprehensive support for creating and working with regions of interest (ROI).

## Creating a spherical ROI

To create a spherical ROI around a central point, we need an existing `NeuroVol` or `NeuroSpace` object.

Basic spherical ROI
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

To create a spherical region of interest with a 5mm radius around a central voxel at (20, 20, 20)</cell id="cell-0">
<cell id="cell-1">import pyneuroim
import numpy as np
# First read in an image to define the space
vol = pyneuroim.read_vol("brain_image.nii.gz")
# Create a spherical ROI centered at voxel [20, 20, 20] with 5mm radius
sphere = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vol, [20, 20, 20], radius=5, fill=100)
print(sphere)
# ROIVol Object
# Properties:
#   Dimensions:  49 x 3
#   ROI Points:  49
#   Value Range: [100.00, 100.00]</cell id="cell-1">
<cell id="cell-2"># Create example data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create example volume
space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), 
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))
example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)
example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())

# Create example 4D data
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100))
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# For tutorials, we'll create objects directly instead of reading files
# vol = pn.read_vol("path/to/image.nii.gz")  # In practice
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)  # For tutorial</cell id="cell-2">
<cell id="cell-3"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>The ``fill`` parameter sets all values in the ROI to 100. If omitted, the ROI will contain the original values from the volume.</cell id="cell-3">
<cell id="cell-4"># Real-world coordinate in mm
rpoint = np.array([-34, -28, 10])
# Convert to voxel coordinates
vox = vol.space.world_to_grid(rpoint.reshape(1, -1))[0]
# Create spherical ROI with 10mm radius
sphere = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vol, vox, radius=10, fill=1)
print(f"ROI contains {len(sphere)} voxels")</cell id="cell-4">
<cell id="cell-5"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Now we can verify the center by converting back to real-world coordinates</cell id="cell-5">
<cell id="cell-6"># Get all coordinates in the ROI
roi_world_coords = sphere.get_coords(real=True)
# Calculate center of mass
center_of_mass = np.mean(roi_world_coords, axis=0)
print(f"Center of mass: {center_of_mass}")</cell id="cell-6">
<cell id="cell-7"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Converting an ROI to a SparseNeuroVol</cell id="cell-7">
<cell id="cell-8"># Create a spherical ROI
sphere = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vol, [50, 10, 10], radius=10, fill=1)
print(sphere)
# Convert to SparseNeuroVol
sparsevol = sphere.as_sparse()
print(f"Sum of ROI values: {np.sum(sparsevol.data)}")
print(f"Sum matches: {np.sum(sparsevol.data) == np.sum(sphere.data)}")
print(f"Dimensions match: {sparsevol.shape == vol.shape}")</cell id="cell-8">
<cell id="cell-9"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Creating other ROI shapes</cell id="cell-9">
<cell id="cell-10"># Create a 7x7 square ROI in the z=10 plane
square = pyneuroim.square_roi(vol, centroid=[30, 30, 10], 
surround=3, fixdim=2, fill=1)
print(f"Square ROI has {len(square)} voxels")</cell id="cell-10">
<cell id="cell-11"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Cuboid ROIs</cell id="cell-11">
<cell id="cell-12"># Create a cuboid with equal dimensions
cube = pyneuroim.cuboid_roi(vol, centroid=[30, 30, 15], 
surround=5, fill=1)
# Create an asymmetric cuboid
cuboid = pyneuroim.cuboid_roi(vol, centroid=[30, 30, 15], 
surround=[3, 4, 5], fill=1)
print(f"Cuboid ROI has {len(cuboid)} voxels")</cell id="cell-12">
<cell id="cell-13"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Working with multiple ROIs</cell id="cell-13">
<cell id="cell-14"># Define multiple ROI centers
centers = np.array([[20, 20, 10],
[40, 40, 15],
[30, 50, 12]])
# Create ROIs with different values
roi_list = pyneuroim.spherical_roi_set(vol, centers, radius=6, 
fill=[100, 200, 300])
print(f"Created {len(roi_list)} ROIs")
for i, roi in enumerate(roi_list):
print(f"ROI {i}: {len(roi)} voxels, value={roi.data[0]}")</cell id="cell-14">
<cell id="cell-15"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Simple searchlight analysis</cell id="cell-15">
<cell id="cell-16"># Generate searchlight ROIs around each voxel
from pyneuroim import searchlight
# Create searchlights with 8mm radius
slist = searchlight(vol, radius=8, mask=vol.data > 0)
# Compute mean value in each searchlight
means = []
for roi in slist:
# Get values from volume at ROI coordinates
values = vol[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
means.append(np.mean(values))
print(f"Computed means for {len(means)} searchlights")</cell id="cell-16">
<cell id="cell-17"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Random searchlight</cell id="cell-17">
<cell id="cell-18">from pyneuroim import random_searchlight
# Create random searchlights
rois = random_searchlight(vol, radius=8, mask=vol.data > 0)
# Process each ROI
results = []
for roi in rois:
values = vol[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
results.append(np.mean(values))</cell id="cell-18">
<cell id="cell-19"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Clustered searchlight</cell id="cell-19">
<cell id="cell-20"># First create a clustering
mask_indices = np.where(vol.data > 0)
coords = np.column_stack(mask_indices)
# Simple k-means clustering
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=50, random_state=42)
labels = kmeans.fit_predict(coords)
# Create ClusteredNeuroVol
cluster_data = np.zeros_like(vol.data)
cluster_data[mask_indices] = labels + 1  # Labels start at 1
kvol = pyneuroim.ClusteredNeuroVol(cluster_data, vol.space)
# Create ROIs from clusters
from pyneuroim import clustered_searchlight
cluster_rois = clustered_searchlight(vol, kvol)
# Analyze each cluster
cluster_means = []
for roi in cluster_rois:
values = vol[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
cluster_means.append(np.mean(values))</cell id="cell-20">
<cell id="cell-21"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>ROI-based time series extraction</cell id="cell-21">
<cell id="cell-22"># Load 4D data
vec = pyneuroim.read_vec("functional_data.nii")
# Create ROI
roi = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vec, [32, 32, 16], radius=10)
# Extract time series for all voxels in ROI
roi_timeseries = vec.series_roi(roi)
print(f"Time series shape: {roi_timeseries.shape}")
# Compute mean time series
mean_ts = np.mean(roi_timeseries, axis=1)
# Plot
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot(mean_ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Mean signal')
plt.title('Mean ROI time series')</cell id="cell-22">
<cell id="cell-23"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Saving and loading ROIs</cell id="cell-23">
<cell id="cell-24"># Create ROI

toolUseResult.source:
# Regions of Interest

In pyneuroim there is comprehensive support for creating and working with regions of interest (ROI).

## Creating a spherical ROI

To create a spherical ROI around a central point, we need an existing `NeuroVol` or `NeuroSpace` object.

Basic spherical ROI
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

To create a spherical region of interest with a 5mm radius around a central voxel at (20, 20, 20)

import pyneuroim
import numpy as np
# First read in an image to define the space
vol = pyneuroim.read_vol("brain_image.nii.gz")
# Create a spherical ROI centered at voxel [20, 20, 20] with 5mm radius
sphere = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vol, [20, 20, 20], radius=5, fill=100)
print(sphere)
# ROIVol Object
# Properties:
#   Dimensions:  49 x 3
#   ROI Points:  49
#   Value Range: [100.00, 100.00]

# Create example data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create example volume
space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), 
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))
example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)
example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())

# Create example 4D data
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100))
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# For tutorials, we'll create objects directly instead of reading files
# vol = pn.read_vol("path/to/image.nii.gz")  # In practice
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)  # For tutorial
The ``fill`` parameter sets all values in the ROI to 100. If omitted, the ROI will contain the original values from the volume.

# Real-world coordinate in mm
rpoint = np.array([-34, -28, 10])
# Convert to voxel coordinates
vox = vol.space.world_to_grid(rpoint.reshape(1, -1))[0]
# Create spherical ROI with 10mm radius
sphere = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vol, vox, radius=10, fill=1)
print(f"ROI contains {len(sphere)} voxels")
Now we can verify the center by converting back to real-world coordinates

# Get all coordinates in the ROI
roi_world_coords = sphere.get_coords(real=True)
# Calculate center of mass
center_of_mass = np.mean(roi_world_coords, axis=0)
print(f"Center of mass: {center_of_mass}")
Converting an ROI to a SparseNeuroVol

# Create a spherical ROI
sphere = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vol, [50, 10, 10], radius=10, fill=1)
print(sphere)
# Convert to SparseNeuroVol
sparsevol = sphere.as_sparse()
print(f"Sum of ROI values: {np.sum(sparsevol.data)}")
print(f"Sum matches: {np.sum(sparsevol.data) == np.sum(sphere.data)}")
print(f"Dimensions match: {sparsevol.shape == vol.shape}")
Creating other ROI shapes

# Create a 7x7 square ROI in the z=10 plane
square = pyneuroim.square_roi(vol, centroid=[30, 30, 10], 
surround=3, fixdim=2, fill=1)
print(f"Square ROI has {len(square)} voxels")
Cuboid ROIs

# Create a cuboid with equal dimensions
cube = pyneuroim.cuboid_roi(vol, centroid=[30, 30, 15], 
surround=5, fill=1)
# Create an asymmetric cuboid
cuboid = pyneuroim.cuboid_roi(vol, centroid=[30, 30, 15], 
surround=[3, 4, 5], fill=1)
print(f"Cuboid ROI has {len(cuboid)} voxels")
Working with multiple ROIs

# Define multiple ROI centers
centers = np.array([[20, 20, 10],
[40, 40, 15],
[30, 50, 12]])
# Create ROIs with different values
roi_list = pyneuroim.spherical_roi_set(vol, centers, radius=6, 
fill=[100, 200, 300])
print(f"Created {len(roi_list)} ROIs")
for i, roi in enumerate(roi_list):
print(f"ROI {i}: {len(roi)} voxels, value={roi.data[0]}")
Simple searchlight analysis

# Generate searchlight ROIs around each voxel
from pyneuroim import searchlight
# Create searchlights with 8mm radius
slist = searchlight(vol, radius=8, mask=vol.data > 0)
# Compute mean value in each searchlight
means = []
for roi in slist:
# Get values from volume at ROI coordinates
values = vol[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
means.append(np.mean(values))
print(f"Computed means for {len(means)} searchlights")
Random searchlight

from pyneuroim import random_searchlight
# Create random searchlights
rois = random_searchlight(vol, radius=8, mask=vol.data > 0)
# Process each ROI
results = []
for roi in rois:
values = vol[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
results.append(np.mean(values))
Clustered searchlight

# First create a clustering
mask_indices = np.where(vol.data > 0)
coords = np.column_stack(mask_indices)
# Simple k-means clustering
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=50, random_state=42)
labels = kmeans.fit_predict(coords)
# Create ClusteredNeuroVol
cluster_data = np.zeros_like(vol.data)
cluster_data[mask_indices] = labels + 1  # Labels start at 1
kvol = pyneuroim.ClusteredNeuroVol(cluster_data, vol.space)
# Create ROIs from clusters
from pyneuroim import clustered_searchlight
cluster_rois = clustered_searchlight(vol, kvol)
# Analyze each cluster
cluster_means = []
for roi in cluster_rois:
values = vol[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
cluster_means.append(np.mean(values))
ROI-based time series extraction

# Load 4D data
vec = pyneuroim.read_vec("functional_data.nii")
# Create ROI
roi = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vec, [32, 32, 16], radius=10)
# Extract time series for all voxels in ROI
roi_timeseries = vec.series_roi(roi)
print(f"Time series shape: {roi_timeseries.shape}")
# Compute mean time series
mean_ts = np.mean(roi_timeseries, axis=1)
# Plot
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot(mean_ts)
plt.xlabel('Time point')
plt.ylabel('Mean signal')
plt.title('Mean ROI time series')
Saving and loading ROIs

# Create ROI
roi = pyneuroim.spherical_roi(vol, [30, 30, 15], radius=8)
# Convert to sparse volume and save
sparse_roi = roi.as_sparse()
pyneuroim.write_vol(sparse_roi, "my_roi.nii.gz")
# Load it back
loaded_roi = pyneuroim.read_vol("my_roi.nii.gz")
# Convert back to ROI if needed
roi_coords = np.column_stack(np.where(loaded_roi.data > 0))
roi_values = loaded_roi.data[loaded_roi.data > 0]
reconstructed_roi = pyneuroim.ROIVol(roi_values, loaded_roi.space, roi_coords)

    "# Create searchlights with 8mm radius\n",
    "slist = searchlight(vol.space, radius=8, mask=mask)\n",

    "# Create random searchlights\n",
    "rois = random_searchlight(vol.space, n=50, radius=8, mask=mask)\n",

    "for roi in rois:\n",

    "    cluster_means.append(np.mean(values))\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Analyzed {len(cluster_means)} clusters\")"

    "plt.show()"
   ]

    "# Regions of Interest\n",
    "\n",
    "In pyneuroim there is comprehensive support for creating and working with regions of interest (ROI).\n",
    "\n",
    "## Creating a spherical ROI\n",
    "\n",
    "To create a spherical ROI around a central point, we need an existing `NeuroVol` or `NeuroSpace` object.\n",
    "\n",
    "### Basic spherical ROI\n",
    "\n",
    "To create a spherical region of interest with a 5mm radius around a central voxel at (20, 20, 20)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Import required libraries\n",
    "import pyneuroim as pn\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create example data for tutorial\n",
    "# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create example volume\n",
    "space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), \n",
    "                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))\n",
    "example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)\n",
    "example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create volume\n",
    "vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)\n",
    "\n",
    "# In practice, you would read a NIFTI file:\n",
    "# vol = pn.read_vol(\"brain_image.nii.gz\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create a spherical ROI centered at voxel [20, 20, 10] with 5mm radius\n",
    "sphere = pn.spherical_roi(vol, [20, 20, 10], radius=5, fill=100)\n",
    "print(sphere)\n",

   ]

<cell id="cell-0"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type># Pipelines and Functional Operations

The pyneuroim package provides a set of functions that allow you to split image data in various ways for processing. By breaking a dataset into pieces, operations can be parallelized and complex analyses can be composed from simple building blocks.

## Splitting an image into connected components

First, let's find connected components in a thresholded volume</cell id="cell-0">
<cell id="cell-1">import pyneuroim
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
# Load an example volume
vol = pyneuroim.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")
# Create random values in the mask
mask_idx = np.where(vol.data > 0)
vol2 = vol.copy()
vol2.data[mask_idx] = np.random.rand(len(mask_idx[0]))
# Find connected components with threshold of 0.8
comp = pyneuroim.conn_comp(vol2, threshold=0.8)
# comp is a ConnCompResult with:
# - comp.index: ClusteredNeuroVol with component labels
# - comp.size: component sizes
# - comp.maxima: local maxima information
print(f"Found {len(comp.size)} connected components")
print(f"Largest component has {max(comp.size)} voxels")
# Visualize the components</cell id="cell-1">
<cell id="cell-2"># Create example data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create example volume
space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), 
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))
example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)
example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())

# Create example 4D data
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100))
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# For tutorials, we'll create objects directly instead of reading files
# vol = pn.read_vol("path/to/image.nii.gz")  # In practice
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)  # For tutorial</cell id="cell-2">
<cell id="cell-3"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Computing statistics on connected components</cell id="cell-3">
<cell id="cell-4"># Calculate mean value in each connected component
from pyneuroim import split_clusters
cluster_rois = split_clusters(vol2, comp.index)
# Compute mean for each cluster
means = [np.mean(roi.data) for roi in cluster_rois]
print(f"Component means: {means[:5]}...")  # Show first 5</cell id="cell-4">
<cell id="cell-5"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Searchlight analysis</cell id="cell-5">
<cell id="cell-6"># Create searchlights with 5mm radius
from pyneuroim import searchlight
# Get all searchlight ROIs
rois = searchlight(vol, radius=5, mask=vol.data > 0)
# Compute standard deviation in each
sd_values = []
indices = []
for roi in rois:
# Extract values from the volume
values = vol2.data[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
sd_values.append(np.std(values))
# Get center voxel index
indices.append(roi.indices()[roi.center_index] if hasattr(roi, 'center_index') else roi.indices()[0])
# Create output volume
sd_vol = pyneuroim.SparseNeuroVol(
data=np.array(sd_values),
space=vol.space,
indices=np.array(indices)
)
print(f"Computed local SD for {len(sd_values)} voxels")</cell id="cell-6">
<cell id="cell-7"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>K-nearest neighbors smoothing</cell id="cell-7">
<cell id="cell-8">k = 12
smoothed_values = []
indices = []
for roi in searchlight(vol2, radius=6, mask=vol2.data > 0):
# Get values in the ROI
roi_coords = roi.coords
roi_values = vol2.data[roi_coords[:, 0], roi_coords[:, 1], roi_coords[:, 2]]
if hasattr(roi, 'center_index'):
center_val = roi_values[roi.center_index]
# Find k nearest neighbors by value
distances = np.abs(roi_values - center_val)
k_nearest_idx = np.argsort(distances)[1:k+1]  # Exclude center itself
smoothed_values.append(np.mean(roi_values[k_nearest_idx]))
else:
# If no center index, just use mean
smoothed_values.append(np.mean(roi_values))
indices.append(roi.indices()[0])
# Create smoothed volume
smoothed_vol = pyneuroim.SparseNeuroVol(
data=np.array(smoothed_values),
space=vol2.space,
indices=np.array(indices)
)</cell id="cell-8">
<cell id="cell-9"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Using searchlight coordinates only</cell id="cell-9">
<cell id="cell-10">from pyneuroim import searchlight_coords
# Get coordinates for each searchlight
avg_values = []
center_indices = []
for coords in searchlight_coords(vol, radius=12, mask=vol.data > 0):
# coords is an Nx3 array of voxel coordinates
vals = vol.data[coords[:, 0], coords[:, 1], coords[:, 2]]
# Average only non-zero values
nonzero_vals = vals[vals != 0]
if len(nonzero_vals) > 0:
avg_values.append(np.mean(nonzero_vals))
# Assume first coordinate is center
center_indices.append(vol.space.grid_to_index(coords[0:1])[0])
# Create averaged volume
avg_vol = pyneuroim.SparseNeuroVol(
data=np.array(avg_values),
space=vol.space,
indices=np.array(center_indices)
)</cell id="cell-10">
<cell id="cell-11"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Processing slices</cell id="cell-11">
<cell id="cell-12"># Process each axial slice
slice_means = []
for z in range(vol.shape[2]):
slice_data = vol.data[:, :, z]
slice_means.append(np.mean(slice_data))
# Plot slice means
plt.plot(slice_means)
plt.xlabel('Slice number')
plt.ylabel('Mean intensity')
plt.title('Mean intensity by slice')
plt.show()</cell id="cell-12">
<cell id="cell-13"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Processing volumes in a 4D dataset</cell id="cell-13">
<cell id="cell-14"># Create a 4D dataset by concatenating volumes
vec = pyneuroim.neurovec([vol, vol, vol, vol, vol])
print(f"4D data shape: {vec.shape}")
# Calculate statistics for each volume
volumes = vec.vols()  # Get list of all volumes
mean_vec = [np.mean(v.data) for v in volumes]
sd_vec = [np.std(v.data) for v in volumes]
print(f"Number of volumes: {len(mean_vec)}")
print(f"Mean values: {mean_vec}")
print(f"SD values: {sd_vec}")</cell id="cell-14">
<cell id="cell-15"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Processing voxel time series</cell id="cell-15">
<cell id="cell-16"># Calculate mean across time for each voxel
vec = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50))
# Method 1: Using numpy operations
mean_vol_data = np.mean(vec.data, axis=3)
mean_vol = pyneuroim.DenseNeuroVol(mean_vol_data, 
pyneuroim.NeuroSpace(dim=mean_vol_data.shape))
mean_values = []
for i in range(vec.shape[0]):
for j in range(vec.shape[1]):
for k in range(vec.shape[2]):
ts = vec.series(i, j, k)
mean_values.append(np.mean(ts))
# Reshape to volume
mean_vol2_data = np.array(mean_values).reshape(vec.shape[:3], order='F')
mean_vol2 = pyneuroim.DenseNeuroVol(mean_vol2_data, mean_vol.space)</cell id="cell-16">
<cell id="cell-17"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Parallel processing</cell id="cell-17">
<cell id="cell-18">from joblib import Parallel, delayed
def process_roi(roi, volume):
"""Process a single ROI"""
values = volume.data[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
return {
'mean': np.mean(values),
'std': np.std(values),
'max': np.max(values),
'size': len(values)
}
# Create ROIs
rois = list(searchlight(vol2, radius=8, mask=vol2.data > 0))
# Process in parallel
results = Parallel(n_jobs=-1)(
delayed(process_roi)(roi, vol2) for roi in rois
)
# Extract results
roi_means = [r['mean'] for r in results]
roi_stds = [r['std'] for r in results]
print(f"Processed {len(results)} ROIs in parallel")</cell id="cell-18">
<cell id="cell-19"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Building analysis pipelines</cell id="cell-19">
<cell id="cell-20">def denoise_pipeline(vol, smooth_radius=5, threshold=0.1):
"""
Example denoising pipeline:
1. Identify noise components
2. Smooth within components
3. Threshold final result
"""
# Find connected components above threshold
comp = pyneuroim.conn_comp(vol, threshold=threshold)
# Process each component
processed_rois = []
for roi in split_clusters(vol, comp.index):
if len(roi) < 10:  # Skip small components
continue
# Smooth within component
roi_coords = roi.coords
values = vol.data[roi_coords[:, 0], roi_coords[:, 1], roi_coords[:, 2]]
# Simple Gaussian smoothing would go here
smoothed_values = values * 0.8  # Placeholder
# Update ROI with smoothed values
roi.data = smoothed_values
processed_rois.append(roi)
# Combine back into volume
result = pyneuroim.DenseNeuroVol(np.zeros_like(vol.data), vol.space)
for roi in processed_rois:
result.data[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]] = roi.data
return result
# Apply pipeline
denoised = denoise_pipeline(vol2, smooth_radius=5, threshold=0.2)</cell id="cell-20">
<cell id="cell-21"><cell_type>markdown</cell_type>Custom iterators</cell id="cell-21">
<cell id="cell-22">def sliding_window_iterator(vec, window_size=10, step=5):
"""
Iterate over overlapping time windows
"""
n_timepoints = vec.shape[3]
for start in range(0, n_timepoints - window_size + 1, step):
end = start + window_size
yield vec.sub_vector(slice(start, end))
# Use the iterator
vec = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 100))
window_means = []
for window_vec in sliding_window_iterator(vec, window_size=20, step=10):
# Calculate mean volume for this time window
mean_vol = np.mean(window_vec.data, axis=3)
window_means.append(mean_vol)
print(f"Computed {len(window_means)} windowed mean volumes")</cell id="cell-22">

# Pipelines and Functional Operations

The pyneuroim package provides a set of functions that allow you to split image data in various ways for processing. By breaking a dataset into pieces, operations can be parallelized and complex analyses can be composed from simple building blocks.

## Splitting an image into connected components

First, let's find connected components in a thresholded volume

import pyneuroim
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
# Load an example volume
vol = pyneuroim.read_vol("brain_mask.nii")
# Create random values in the mask
mask_idx = np.where(vol.data > 0)
vol2 = vol.copy()
vol2.data[mask_idx] = np.random.rand(len(mask_idx[0]))
# Find connected components with threshold of 0.8
comp = pyneuroim.conn_comp(vol2, threshold=0.8)
# comp is a ConnCompResult with:
# - comp.index: ClusteredNeuroVol with component labels
# - comp.size: component sizes
# - comp.maxima: local maxima information
print(f"Found {len(comp.size)} connected components")
print(f"Largest component has {max(comp.size)} voxels")
# Visualize the components

# Create example data for tutorial
# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)

# Create example volume
space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), 
                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))
example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)
example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())

# Create example 4D data
space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25, 100))
example_4d_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25, 100)

# For tutorials, we'll create objects directly instead of reading files
# vol = pn.read_vol("path/to/image.nii.gz")  # In practice
vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)  # For tutorial
Computing statistics on connected components

# Calculate mean value in each connected component
from pyneuroim import split_clusters
cluster_rois = split_clusters(vol2, comp.index)
# Compute mean for each cluster
means = [np.mean(roi.data) for roi in cluster_rois]
print(f"Component means: {means[:5]}...")  # Show first 5
Searchlight analysis

# Create searchlights with 5mm radius
from pyneuroim import searchlight
# Get all searchlight ROIs
rois = searchlight(vol, radius=5, mask=vol.data > 0)
# Compute standard deviation in each
sd_values = []
indices = []
for roi in rois:
# Extract values from the volume
values = vol2.data[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
sd_values.append(np.std(values))
# Get center voxel index
indices.append(roi.indices()[roi.center_index] if hasattr(roi, 'center_index') else roi.indices()[0])
# Create output volume
sd_vol = pyneuroim.SparseNeuroVol(
data=np.array(sd_values),
space=vol.space,
indices=np.array(indices)
)
print(f"Computed local SD for {len(sd_values)} voxels")
K-nearest neighbors smoothing

k = 12
smoothed_values = []
indices = []
for roi in searchlight(vol2, radius=6, mask=vol2.data > 0):
# Get values in the ROI
roi_coords = roi.coords
roi_values = vol2.data[roi_coords[:, 0], roi_coords[:, 1], roi_coords[:, 2]]
if hasattr(roi, 'center_index'):
center_val = roi_values[roi.center_index]
# Find k nearest neighbors by value
distances = np.abs(roi_values - center_val)
k_nearest_idx = np.argsort(distances)[1:k+1]  # Exclude center itself
smoothed_values.append(np.mean(roi_values[k_nearest_idx]))
else:
# If no center index, just use mean
smoothed_values.append(np.mean(roi_values))
indices.append(roi.indices()[0])
# Create smoothed volume
smoothed_vol = pyneuroim.SparseNeuroVol(
data=np.array(smoothed_values),
space=vol2.space,
indices=np.array(indices)
)
Using searchlight coordinates only

from pyneuroim import searchlight_coords
# Get coordinates for each searchlight
avg_values = []
center_indices = []
for coords in searchlight_coords(vol, radius=12, mask=vol.data > 0):
# coords is an Nx3 array of voxel coordinates
vals = vol.data[coords[:, 0], coords[:, 1], coords[:, 2]]
# Average only non-zero values
nonzero_vals = vals[vals != 0]
if len(nonzero_vals) > 0:
avg_values.append(np.mean(nonzero_vals))
# Assume first coordinate is center
center_indices.append(vol.space.grid_to_index(coords[0:1])[0])
# Create averaged volume
avg_vol = pyneuroim.SparseNeuroVol(
data=np.array(avg_values),
space=vol.space,
indices=np.array(center_indices)
)
Processing slices

# Process each axial slice
slice_means = []
for z in range(vol.shape[2]):
slice_data = vol.data[:, :, z]
slice_means.append(np.mean(slice_data))
# Plot slice means
plt.plot(slice_means)
plt.xlabel('Slice number')
plt.ylabel('Mean intensity')
plt.title('Mean intensity by slice')
plt.show()
Processing volumes in a 4D dataset

# Create a 4D dataset by concatenating volumes
vec = pyneuroim.neurovec([vol, vol, vol, vol, vol])
print(f"4D data shape: {vec.shape}")
# Calculate statistics for each volume
volumes = vec.vols()  # Get list of all volumes
mean_vec = [np.mean(v.data) for v in volumes]
sd_vec = [np.std(v.data) for v in volumes]
print(f"Number of volumes: {len(mean_vec)}")
print(f"Mean values: {mean_vec}")
print(f"SD values: {sd_vec}")
Processing voxel time series

# Calculate mean across time for each voxel
vec = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50))
# Method 1: Using numpy operations
mean_vol_data = np.mean(vec.data, axis=3)
mean_vol = pyneuroim.DenseNeuroVol(mean_vol_data, 
pyneuroim.NeuroSpace(dim=mean_vol_data.shape))
mean_values = []
for i in range(vec.shape[0]):
for j in range(vec.shape[1]):
for k in range(vec.shape[2]):
ts = vec.series(i, j, k)
mean_values.append(np.mean(ts))
# Reshape to volume
mean_vol2_data = np.array(mean_values).reshape(vec.shape[:3], order='F')
mean_vol2 = pyneuroim.DenseNeuroVol(mean_vol2_data, mean_vol.space)
Parallel processing

from joblib import Parallel, delayed
def process_roi(roi, volume):
"""Process a single ROI"""
values = volume.data[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]
return {
'mean': np.mean(values),
'std': np.std(values),
'max': np.max(values),
'size': len(values)
}
# Create ROIs
rois = list(searchlight(vol2, radius=8, mask=vol2.data > 0))
# Process in parallel
results = Parallel(n_jobs=-1)(
delayed(process_roi)(roi, vol2) for roi in rois
)
# Extract results
roi_means = [r['mean'] for r in results]
roi_stds = [r['std'] for r in results]
print(f"Processed {len(results)} ROIs in parallel")
Building analysis pipelines

def denoise_pipeline(vol, smooth_radius=5, threshold=0.1):
"""
Example denoising pipeline:
1. Identify noise components
2. Smooth within components
3. Threshold final result
"""
# Find connected components above threshold
comp = pyneuroim.conn_comp(vol, threshold=threshold)
# Process each component
processed_rois = []
for roi in split_clusters(vol, comp.index):
if len(roi) < 10:  # Skip small components
continue
# Smooth within component
roi_coords = roi.coords
values = vol.data[roi_coords[:, 0], roi_coords[:, 1], roi_coords[:, 2]]
# Simple Gaussian smoothing would go here
smoothed_values = values * 0.8  # Placeholder
# Update ROI with smoothed values
roi.data = smoothed_values
processed_rois.append(roi)
# Combine back into volume
result = pyneuroim.DenseNeuroVol(np.zeros_like(vol.data), vol.space)
for roi in processed_rois:
result.data[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]] = roi.data
return result
# Apply pipeline
denoised = denoise_pipeline(vol2, smooth_radius=5, threshold=0.2)
Custom iterators

def sliding_window_iterator(vec, window_size=10, step=5):
"""
Iterate over overlapping time windows
"""
n_timepoints = vec.shape[3]
for start in range(0, n_timepoints - window_size + 1, step):
end = start + window_size
yield vec.sub_vector(slice(start, end))
# Use the iterator
vec = pyneuroim.neurovec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 100))
window_means = []
for window_vec in sliding_window_iterator(vec, window_size=20, step=10):
# Calculate mean volume for this time window
mean_vol = np.mean(window_vec.data, axis=3)
window_means.append(mean_vol)
print(f"Computed {len(window_means)} windowed mean volumes")

   "cell_type": "code",

   "source": [
    "# Create a 4D dataset\n",

    "\n",
    "print(f\"4D data shape: {vec.shape}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Calculate statistics for each volume\n",
    "volumes = vec.vols()  # Get list of all volumes\n",
    "mean_vec = [np.mean(v.data) for v in volumes]\n",
    "sd_vec = [np.std(v.data) for v in volumes]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Number of volumes: {len(mean_vec)}\")\n",

   "cell_type": "markdown",

   "source": [
    "## Processing voxel time series"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Calculate mean across time for each voxel\n",
    "space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(10, 10, 10, 50))\n",
    "vec = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50), space_4d)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Method 1: Using numpy operations\n",
    "mean_vol_data = np.mean(vec.data, axis=3)\n",
    "mean_vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(mean_vol_data, vec.space.sub_space())\n",
    "\n",
    "# Method 2: Using voxel iteration\n",
    "mean_values = []\n",
    "for i in range(vec.shape[0]):\n",
    "    for j in range(vec.shape[1]):\n",
    "        for k in range(vec.shape[2]):\n",
    "            ts = vec.series(i, j, k)\n",
    "            mean_values.append(np.mean(ts))\n",
    "\n",
    "# Reshape to volume\n",
    "mean_vol2_data = np.array(mean_values).reshape(vec.shape[:3], order='F')\n",
    "mean_vol2 = pn.DenseNeuroVol(mean_vol2_data, mean_vol.space)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Mean volume shape: {mean_vol.shape}\")"
   ]

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Parallel processing"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "from joblib import Parallel, delayed\n",
    "\n",
    "def process_roi(roi, volume):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Process a single ROI\"\"\"\n",
    "    values = volume[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]\n",
    "    return {\n",
    "        'mean': np.mean(values),\n",
    "        'std': np.std(values),\n",
    "        'max': np.max(values),\n",
    "        'size': len(values)\n",
    "    }\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create ROIs (limit for demo)\n",
    "rois = list(searchlight(vol2.space, radius=8, mask=mask))[:20]\n",
    "\n",
    "# Process in parallel\n",
    "results = Parallel(n_jobs=-1)(\n",
    "    delayed(process_roi)(roi, vol2) for roi in rois\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Extract results\n",
    "roi_means = [r['mean'] for r in results]\n",
    "roi_stds = [r['std'] for r in results]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Processed {len(results)} ROIs in parallel\")\n",
    "print(f\"Mean values range: [{min(roi_means):.3f}, {max(roi_means):.3f}]\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Building analysis pipelines"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "def denoise_pipeline(vol, smooth_radius=5, threshold=0.1):\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    Example denoising pipeline:\n",
    "    1. Identify noise components\n",
    "    2. Smooth within components\n",
    "    3. Threshold final result\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    # Find connected components above threshold\n",
    "    comp = pn.conn_comp(vol, threshold=threshold)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Process each component\n",
    "    processed_rois = []\n",
    "    for roi in split_clusters(vol, comp.index):\n",
    "        if len(roi) < 10:  # Skip small components\n",
    "            continue\n",
    "            \n",
    "        # Smooth within component\n",
    "        roi_coords = roi.coords\n",
    "        values = vol[roi_coords[:, 0], roi_coords[:, 1], roi_coords[:, 2]]\n",
    "        \n",
    "        # Simple smoothing: multiply by factor\n",
    "        smoothed_values = values * 0.8\n",
    "        \n",
    "        # Update ROI with smoothed values\n",
    "        roi.data = smoothed_values\n",
    "        processed_rois.append(roi)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Combine back into volume\n",
    "    result = pn.DenseNeuroVol(np.zeros_like(vol.data), vol.space)\n",
    "    for roi in processed_rois:\n",
    "        result[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]] = roi.data\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return result\n",
    "\n",
    "# Apply pipeline\n",
    "denoised = denoise_pipeline(vol2, smooth_radius=5, threshold=0.2)\n",
    "print(f\"Denoised volume range: [{denoised.min():.3f}, {denoised.max():.3f}]\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Custom iterators"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "def sliding_window_iterator(vec, window_size=10, step=5):\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    Iterate over overlapping time windows\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    n_timepoints = vec.shape[3]\n",
    "    for start in range(0, n_timepoints - window_size + 1, step):\n",
    "        end = start + window_size\n",
    "        yield vec.sub_vector(slice(start, end))\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create 4D data\n",
    "space_4d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(10, 10, 10, 100))\n",
    "vec = pn.DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 100), space_4d)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Use the iterator\n",
    "window_means = []\n",
    "for window_vec in sliding_window_iterator(vec, window_size=20, step=10):\n",
    "    # Calculate mean volume for this time window\n",
    "    mean_vol = np.mean(window_vec.data, axis=3)\n",
    "    window_means.append(mean_vol)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Computed {len(window_means)} windowed mean volumes\")\n",
    "print(f\"Each window mean volume has shape: {window_means[0].shape}\")"
   ]
  }

    "# Pipelines and Functional Operations\n",
    "\n",
    "The pyneuroim package provides a set of functions that allow you to split image data in various ways for processing. By breaking a dataset into pieces, operations can be parallelized and complex analyses can be composed from simple building blocks.\n",
    "\n",
    "## Splitting an image into connected components\n",
    "\n",
    "First, let's find connected components in a thresholded volume"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Import required libraries\n",
    "import pyneuroim as pn\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create example data for tutorial\n",
    "# (In practice, you would use real neuroimaging files)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create example volume\n",
    "space_3d = pn.NeuroSpace(dim=(64, 64, 25), spacing=(3.5, 3.5, 5.0), \n",
    "                         origin=(-110.5, -88.9342, -42.75))\n",
    "example_data = np.random.randn(64, 64, 25)\n",
    "example_data = (example_data - example_data.min()) / (example_data.max() - example_data.min())\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create volume\n",
    "vol = pn.DenseNeuroVol(example_data, space_3d)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create another volume with higher values in some regions\n",
    "vol2 = vol.as_dense()\n",
    "# Add some high-value regions\n",
    "vol2.data[20:30, 20:30, 10:15] = 0.9\n",
    "vol2.data[40:50, 40:50, 15:20] = 0.85\n",
    "\n",
    "# Find connected components with threshold of 0.8\n",
    "comp = pn.conn_comp(vol2, threshold=0.8)\n",
    "\n",
    "# comp is a ConnCompResult with:\n",
    "# - comp.index: ClusteredNeuroVol with component labels\n",
    "# - comp.size: component sizes\n",
    "# - comp.maxima: local maxima information\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Found {len(comp.size)} connected components\")\n",
    "print(f\"Largest component has {max(comp.size)} voxels\")"
   ]

    "\n",
    "# Compute mean for each cluster\n",
    "means = [np.mean(roi.data) for roi in cluster_rois]\n",
    "print(f\"Component means: {means[:5]}...\")  # Show first 5"
